problems.explanationZh:
6. 电子邮件按照参数为 λ 的泊松过程到达收件箱（故任意长度为 t 的时间间隔内邮件数量分布为 Pois(λt)，且不相交时间间隔的邮件数量相互独立）。设 X, Y, Z 分别为某天9点至中午、中午至18点、18点至午夜的邮件数。
(a) 求 X, Y, Z 的联合概率质量函数。
由于 X ∼ Pois(3λ), Y ∼ Pois(6λ), Z ∼ Pois(6λ) 且相互独立，联合概率质量函数为：
\[
P(X = x, Y = y, Z = z) = e^{-3\lambda} \frac{(3\lambda)^x}{x!} \times e^{-6\lambda} \frac{(6\lambda)^y}{y!} \times e^{-6\lambda} \frac{(6\lambda)^z}{z!},
\]
其中 x, y, z 均为非负整数。
(b) 求在 X + Y + Z = 36 条件下的条件联合概率质量函数。
设 T = X + Y + Z ∼ Pois(15λ)，观测到 T = t。若 x + y + z ≠ t，则条件概率为 0。若 x + y + z = t，则有
\[
P(X=x, Y=y, Z=z | T=t) = \frac{P(X=x, Y=y, Z=z)}{P(T=t)} = \frac{e^{-3\lambda} (3\lambda)^x / x! \times e^{-6\lambda} (6\lambda)^y / y! \times e^{-6\lambda} (6\lambda)^z / z!}{e^{-15\lambda} (15\lambda)^t / t!} = \frac{t!}{x! y! z!} \left( \frac{3}{15} \right)^x \left( \frac{6}{15} \right)^y \left( \frac{6}{15} \right)^z.
\]
因此，给定 T = t，(X, Y, Z) 服从多项式分布 Multinomial(t, (3/15, 6/15, 6/15))。当 t=36 时，即为
Multinomial(36, (1/5, 2/5, 2/5))。
(c) 求在 X + Y + Z = 36 条件下 X + Y 的条件概率质量函数，并求条件期望和方差。
令 W = X + Y，T = X + Y + Z。根据多项式分布与题(b)的结论，合并第一区间和第二区间后，条件分布为 Binomial(36, 9/15)。
因此
\[ E(W|T=36) = 36 \times \frac{9}{15} = 21.6, \quad Var(W|T=36) = 36 \times \frac{9}{15} \times \frac{6}{15} = 8.64. \]

problems.titleZh: 作业7 - 作业7 第7题

classificationReview.logic: Harvard Stat 110 is a probability course. Difficulty is inferred from set number, homework/strategic role, proof wording, and extracted prompt/solution length.

problems.bookName: Harvard Stat 110 概率论战略练习 Statistics 110 Strategic Practice
problems.promptEn:
7. Shakespeare wrote a total of 884647 words in his known works. Of course, many
words are used more than once, and the number of distinct words in Shakespeare's
known writings is 31534 (according to one computation). This puts a lower bound
on the size of Shakespeare's vocabulary, but it is likely that Shakespeare knew words
which he did not use in these known writings.
More specifically, suppose that a new poem of Shakespeare were uncovered, and
consider the following (seemingly impossible) problem: give a good prediction of the
number of words in the new poem that do not appear anywhere in Shakespeare's
previously known works.
The statisticians Ronald Thisted and Bradley Efron studied this problem in a
paper called "Did Shakespeare write a newly-discovered poem?", which performed
statistical tests to try to determine whether Shakespeare was the author of a poem
discovered by a Shakespearean scholar in 1985. A simplified version of their method
is developed in the problem below. The method was originally invented by Alan
Turing (the founder of computer science) and I.J. Good as part of the eort to break
the German Enigma code during World War II.
Let N be the number of distinct words that Shakespeare knew, and assume these
words are numbered from 1 to N. Suppose for simplicity that Shakespeare wrote
only two plays, A and B. The plays are reasonably long and they are of the same
length. Let Xj be the number of times that word j appears in play A, and Yj be the
number of times it appears in play B, for 1 j N.
(a) Explain why it is reasonable to model Xj as being Poisson, and Yj as being
Poisson with the same parameter as Xj.

(b) Let the numbers of occurrences of the word "eyeball" (which was coined by
Shakespare) in the two plays be independent Pois(λ) r.v.s. Show that the probability
that "eyeball" is used in play B but not in play A is
e-λ(λ -λ2/2! + λ3/3! -λ4/4! + . . . ).
(c) Now assume that λ from (b) is unknown and is itself taken to be a random
variable to reflect this uncertainty. So let λ have a PDF f0. Let X be the number
of times the word "eyeball" appears in play A and Y be the corresponding value for
play B. Assume that the conditional distribution of X, Y given λ is that they are
independent Pois(λ) r.v.s. Show that the probability that "eyeball" is used in play
B but not in play A is the alternating series
P(X = 1) -P(X = 2) + P(X = 3) -P(X = 4) + . . . .
Hint: condition on λ and use (b).
(d) Assume that every word's numbers of occurrences in A and B are distributed
as in (c), where λ may be dierent for dierent words but f0 is fixed. Let Wj be
the number of words that appear exactly j times in play A. Show that the expected
number of distinct words appearing in play B but not in play A is
E(W1) -E(W2) + E(W3) -E(W4) + . . . .
(This shows that W1 -W2 + W3 -W4 + . . . is an unbiased predictor of the number
of distinct words appearing in play B but not in play A: on average it is correct.
Moreover, it can be computed just from having seen play A, without needing to know
f0 or any of the λj. This method can be extended in various ways to give predictions
for unobserved plays based on observed plays.)
problems.promptZh:
7. 莎士比亚已知作品中共写了884647个单词。当然，很多单词出现多次，而莎士比亚已知著作中的不同单词数为31534（据一项统计）。这给他的词汇量设定了下界，但莎士比亚很可能知道一些他未曾使用的单词。
更具体地，假设发现了一首新的莎士比亚诗歌，考虑以下看似不可能的问题：如何合理预测这首新诗中未曾出现在莎士比亚已知作品中的单词数量。
统计学家 Ronald Thisted 和 Bradley Efron 在论文“莎士比亚写过一首新发现的诗吗？”中研究了这个问题，用统计检验尝试判断1985年一位莎士比亚学者发现的一首诗是否确为莎士比亚所写。下面题目简化了该方法。该方法最初由计算机科学创始人艾伦·图灵 (Alan Turing) 和 I.J. Good 提出，用于第二次世界大战期间破解德国恩尼格码密码。
设 N 为莎士比亚所知单词的不同词数，词编号为1到N。为简化，假设莎士比亚只写了两部剧本 A 和 B，两部剧本长度相近。令 X_j 表示单词 j 在剧本 A 中出现次数，Y_j 表示单词 j 在剧本 B 中出现次数，j=1,…,N。
(a) 说明为何将 X_j 建模为泊松分布，且 Y_j 同参数且独立地服从泊松分布是合理的。
(b) 假设新造词 “eyeball” 在两部剧本中出现次数为相互独立的 Pois(λ) 随机变量。证明 "eyeball" 在剧本 B 中使用但未在剧本 A 中使用的概率为
\[ e^{-\lambda} (\lambda - \frac{\lambda^2}{2!} + \frac{\lambda^3}{3!} - \frac{\lambda^4}{4!} + \cdots ). \]
(c) 现假设 λ 未知且自身为一个随机变量，概率密度函数为 f_0。设 X, Y 分别是该词在剧本 A 和 B 中的出现次数。假定在给定 λ 条件下 X, Y 独立且服从 Pois(λ)。证明 "eyeball" 在 B 中使用但未在 A 中使用的概率为交替级数
\[ P(X=1) - P(X=2) + P(X=3) - P(X=4) + \cdots. \]
提示：对 λ 条件化并利用(b)的结果。
(d) 假设所有单词在 A、B 中出现次数均按(c)的分布，λ 可不同但 f_0 固定。令 W_j 表示在剧本 A 中恰好出现 j 次的单词数。证明 B 中出现但 A 中未出现的不同单词数的期望为
\[ E(W_1) - E(W_2) + E(W_3) - E(W_4) + \cdots. \]
（这表明 W_1 - W_2 + W_3 - W_4 + ··· 是 B 中出现而 A 中未出现的单词数的无偏估计，仅需 A 的观测数据，无需 f_0 或各 λ_j，且可推广用于基于观测剧本预测未观测剧本的词汇。）。
problems.explanation:
7. Shakespeare wrote a total of 884647 words in his known works. Of course, many
words are used more than once, and the number of distinct words in Shakespeare's
known writings is 31534 (according to one computation). This puts a lower bound
on the size of Shakespeare's vocabulary, but it is likely that Shakespeare knew words
which he did not use in these known writings.
More specifically, suppose that a new poem of Shakespeare were uncovered, and
consider the following (seemingly impossible) problem: give a good prediction of the
number of words in the new poem that do not appear anywhere in Shakespeare's
previously known works.
The statisticians Ronald Thisted and Bradley Efron studied this problem in a
paper called "Did Shakespeare write a newly-discovered poem?", which performed
statistical tests to try to determine whether Shakespeare was the author of a poem
discovered by a Shakespearean scholar in 1985. A simplified version of their method
is developed in the problem below. The method was originally invented by Alan
Turing (the founder of computer science) and I.J. Good as part of the eort to break
the German Enigma code during World War II.
Let N be the number of distinct words that Shakespeare knew, and assume these
words are numbered from 1 to N. Suppose for simplicity that Shakespeare wrote
only two plays, A and B. The plays are reasonably long and they are of the same
length. Let Xj be the number of times that word j appears in play A, and Yj be the
number of times it appears in play B, for 1 j N.
(a) Explain why it is reasonable to model Xj as being Poisson, and Yj as being
Poisson with the same parameter as Xj.
It is reasonable to model Xj and Yj as Poisson, because this distribution is used
to describe the number of "events" (such as emails received) happening at some
average rate in a fixed interval or volume. The Poisson paradigm applies here: each

individual word in a play has some very small probability of being word j, and the
words are weakly dependent. Here an event means using word j, the average rate is
determined by how frequently Shakespeare uses that word overall. It is reasonable
to assume that the average rate of occurrence of a particular word is the same for
two plays by the same author, so we take λ to be the same for Xj and Yj.
(b) Let the numbers of occurrences of the word "eyeball" (which was coined by
Shakespare) in the two plays be independent Pois(λ) r.v.s. Show that the probability
that "eyeball" is used in play B but not in play A is
e-λ(λ -λ2/2! + λ3/3! -λ4/4! + . . . ).
Let X be the number of times that "eyeball" is used in play A, and Y be the
number of times that it is used in play B. Since X and Y are independent Pois(λ),

(c) Now assume that λ from (b) is unknown and is itself taken to be a random
variable to reflect this uncertainty. So let λ have a PDF f0. Let X be the number
of times the word "eyeball" appears in play A and Y be the corresponding value for
play B. Assume that the conditional distribution of X, Y given λ is that they are
independent Pois(λ) r.v.s. Show that the probability that "eyeball" is used in play
B but not in play A is the alternating series
P(X = 1) -P(X = 2) + P(X = 3) -P(X = 4) + . . . .
Hint: condition on λ and use (b).
Now λ is a random variable. Given λ, the calculation from (b) holds. By the law
of total probability,

(d) Assume that every word's numbers of occurrences in A and B are distributed
as in (c), where λ may be dierent for dierent words but f0 is fixed. Let Wj be
the number of words that appear exactly j times in play A. Show that the expected
number of distinct words appearing in play B but not in play A is
E(W1) -E(W2) + E(W3) -E(W4) + . . . .
(This shows that W1 -W2 + . . . is an unbiased prediction of the number of distinct
words appearing in play B but not in play A: on average it is correct. Moreover,
it can be computed just from having seen play A, without needing to know f0 or
any of the λj. This method can be extended in various ways to give predictions for
unobserved plays based on observed plays.)

problems.explanationZh:
7. 莎士比亚已知作品共写了884647个单词，很多单词重复出现。据统计，他已知作品中不同单词数量为31534，这给他的词汇量设下了下界，但他很可能还知道未曾使用的词汇。
假设发现一首新的莎士比亚诗歌，考虑如何合理预测该诗中未出现在已知著作中的单词数。
统计学家 Ronald Thisted 和 Bradley Efron 在论文“莎士比亚写过一首新发现的诗吗？”中探讨该问题，使用统计方法试图判断一首1985年被发现的诗歌是否莎士比亚所写。该方法简化版见下。该方法由计算机科学奠基人艾伦·图灵和 I.J. Good 发明，用于二战中破解恩尼格码。
设 N 为莎士比亚所知的不同单词数，单词编号1至N。简化假设莎士比亚只写了两部长度相近的剧本 A 和 B。令 X_j 为单词 j 在 A 中出现次数，Y_j 在 B 中出现次数，j=1,...,N。
(a) 说明为何将 X_j 和 Y_j 建模为参数相同的独立泊松随机变量合理。
泊松分布用于计数某类事件在固定区间或体积以平均速率发生的次数。这里，每个单词 j 出现是“事件”，平均速率由莎士比亚使用该词的频率决定。由于单词独立性及出现率低，模型符合泊松过程特征。因同一作者的两部剧本长度相近，合理假定相同词语出现率 λ 相同，故 X_j 和 Y_j 独立且都服从 Pois(λ)。
(b) 假设词 “eyeball”（莎士比亚新造词）在两剧本中出现次数独立 Pois(λ) 变量，证明 “eyeball” 在 B 中出现但 A 中未出现的概率为
\[ e^{-\lambda} (\lambda - \frac{\lambda^2}{2!} + \frac{\lambda^3}{3!} - \frac{\lambda^4}{4!} + \cdots ). \]
设 X 为 A 中该词出现次数，Y 为 B 中。X, Y 独立，且都是 Pois(λ)，故
\[
P(X=0, Y>0) = P(X=0)(1 - P(Y=0)) = e^{-\lambda} (1 - e^{-\lambda}) = e^{-\lambda} \left(1 - e^{-\lambda} \right).
\]
由泰勒级数展开，
e^{-\lambda} = 1 - \lambda + \frac{\lambda^2}{2!} - \frac{\lambda^3}{3!} + \cdots，代入后得所示展开式。
(c) 现假设 λ 本身为随机变量，密度为 f_0，且条件下 X, Y 独立 Pois(λ)。证明 “eyeball” 在 B 中出现但未出现在 A 中的概率为
\[ P(X=1) - P(X=2) + P(X=3) - P(X=4) + \cdots. \]
由全概率公式：
\[
P(X=0, Y>0) = \int P(X=0, Y>0 | \lambda) f_0(\lambda) d\lambda.
\]
利用(b)的结果，得
\[
= \int e^{-\lambda} (\lambda - \frac{\lambda^2}{2!} + \frac{\lambda^3}{3!} - \frac{\lambda^4}{4!} + \cdots) f_0(\lambda)d\lambda =
\sum_{i=1}^\infty (-1)^{i+1} P(X = i).
\]
(d) 假设每个单词 j 在 A、B 中的出现次数都服从(c)中描述的分布，λ 对不同单词可能不同但密度 f_0 固定。令 W_j 为在 A 中恰好出现 j 次的不同单词数。证明 B 中出现且 A 中未出现的不同单词数的期望为
\[ E(W_1) - E(W_2) + E(W_3) - E(W_4) + \cdots. \]
令 I_j = 指示变量，表示词 j 在 B 中出现且未在 A 中出现。
则 B 中仅在 B 中出现的词数为 W = \sum_{j=1}^N I_j，期望为
\[
E(W) = \sum_{j=1}^N E(I_j) = \sum_{j=1}^N \sum_{i=1}^\infty (-1)^{i+1} P(X_j = i) = \sum_{i=1}^\infty (-1)^{i+1} \sum_{j=1}^N P(X_j = i).
\]
因为
\[
W_i = \text{剧本 A 中恰好出现 } i \text{ 次的单词数} = \sum_{j=1}^N I(X_j = i),
\]
且
\[
E(W_i) = \sum_{j=1}^N P(X_j = i),
\]
因此
\[
E(W) = E(W_1) - E(W_2) + E(W_3) - E(W_4) + \cdots.
\]
该式表明，序列 W_1 - W_2 + W_3 - W_4 + ··· 是关于“B 中出现而 A 中未出现的词数”的无偏估计，只需 A 中单词出现次数即可计算，且不依赖于未知的 f_0 或 λ_j，可推广用于基于已知文本预测新文本词汇。

problems.titleZh: 策略练习 8 - 协方差与相关性 #1

classificationReview.logic: Harvard Stat 110 is a probability course. Difficulty is inferred from set number, homework/strategic role, proof wording, and extracted prompt/solution length.

problems.bookName: Harvard Stat 110 概率论战略练习 Statistics 110 Strategic Practice
problems.promptEn:
1. Two fair six-sided dice are rolled (one green and one orange), with outcomes
X and Y respectively for the green and the orange.
(a) Compute the covariance of X + Y and X -Y .
(b) Are X + Y and X -Y independent? Show that they are, or that they
aren't (whichever is true).
problems.promptZh:
1. 投掷两颗公平的六面骰子（一颗绿色，一颗橙色），绿色骰子的结果为 X，橙色骰子的结果为 Y。
(a) 计算 X + Y 与 X - Y 的协方差。
(b) X + Y 和 X - Y 是否相互独立？请证明它们是否独立（无论哪个为真）。
problems.explanation:
1. Two fair six-sided dice are rolled (one green and one orange), with outcomes
X and Y respectively for the green and the orange.
(a) Compute the covariance of X + Y and X -Y .
Cov(X + Y, X -Y ) = Cov(X, X) -Cov(X, Y ) + Cov(Y, X) -Cov(Y, Y ) = 0.
(b) Are X + Y and X -Y independent? Show that they are, or that they
aren't (whichever is true).
They are not independent: information about X + Y may give information
about X -Y , as shown by considering an extreme example.
Note that if
X + Y = 12, then X = Y = 6, so X -Y = 0.
Therefore, P(X -Y =
0|X + Y = 12) = 1 6= P(X -Y = 0), which shows that X + Y and X -Y are
not independent. Alternatively, note that X + Y and X -Y are both even or
both odd, since the dierence X + Y -(X -Y ) = 2Y is even.
problems.explanationEn:
1. Two fair six-sided dice are rolled (one green and one orange), with outcomes
X and Y respectively for the green and the orange.
(a) Compute the covariance of X + Y and X -Y .
Cov(X + Y, X -Y ) = Cov(X, X) -Cov(X, Y ) + Cov(Y, X) -Cov(Y, Y ) = 0.
(b) Are X + Y and X -Y independent? Show that they are, or that they
aren't (whichever is true).
They are not independent: information about X + Y may give information
about X -Y , as shown by considering an extreme example.
Note that if
X + Y = 12, then X = Y = 6, so X -Y = 0.
Therefore, P(X -Y =
0|X + Y = 12) = 1 6= P(X -Y = 0), which shows that X + Y and X -Y are
not independent. Alternatively, note that X + Y and X -Y are both even or
both odd, since the dierence X + Y -(X -Y ) = 2Y is even.
problems.explanationZh:
1. 投掷两颗公平的六面骰子（一颗绿色，一颗橙色），绿色骰子的结果为 X，橙色骰子的结果为 Y。
(a) 计算 X + Y 与 X - Y 的协方差。
Cov(X + Y, X - Y) = Cov(X, X) - Cov(X, Y) + Cov(Y, X) - Cov(Y, Y) = 0。
(b) X + Y 和 X - Y 是否相互独立？请证明它们是否独立（无论哪个为真）。
它们不独立：关于 X + Y 的信息可能会提供关于 X - Y 的信息，可以通过一个极端的例子来看出。
注意，若 X + Y = 12，则 X = Y = 6，因此 X - Y = 0。
因此，P(X - Y = 0 | X + Y = 12) = 1 ≠ P(X - Y = 0)，这说明 X + Y 和 X - Y 不独立。
或者，注意到 X + Y 和 X - Y 要么同时为偶数，要么同时为奇数，因为差值 X + Y - (X - Y) = 2Y 是偶数。

problems.titleZh: 策略练习 8 - 协方差与相关性 #2

classificationReview.logic: Harvard Stat 110 is a probability course. Difficulty is inferred from set number, homework/strategic role, proof wording, and extracted prompt/solution length.

problems.bookName: Harvard Stat 110 概率论战略练习 Statistics 110 Strategic Practice
problems.promptEn:
2. A chicken lays a Poisson(λ) number N of eggs.
Each egg, independently,
hatches a chick with probability p.
Let X be the number which hatch, so
X|N ⇠Bin(N, p).
Find the correlation between N (the number of eggs) and X (the number of
eggs which hatch). Simplify; your final answer should work out to a simple
function of p (the λ should cancel out).
problems.promptZh:
2. 一只母鸡产下的蛋数 N 服从 Poisson(λ)。
每个蛋孵化成小鸡的概率为 p，且各蛋孵化情况相互独立。
令 X 为孵化成功的小鸡数量，因此条件于 N 时，X|N ∼ Binomial(N, p)。
求蛋的数量 N 与孵化成功数 X 之间的相关系数。请简化结果，你的最终答案应仅依赖于 p（λ 会被抵消）。
problems.explanation:
2. A chicken lays a Poisson(λ) number N of eggs.
Each egg, independently,
hatches a chick with probability p.
Let X be the number which hatch, so
X|N ⇠Bin(N, p).
Find the correlation between N (the number of eggs) and X (the number of
eggs which hatch). Simplify; your final answer should work out to a simple
function of p (the λ should cancel out).
As shown in class, in this story X is independent of Y , with X ⇠Pois(λp) and
Y ⇠Pois(λq), for q = 1 -p. So
Cov(N, X) = Cov(X + Y, X) = Cov(X, X) + Cov(Y, X) = Var(X) = λp,
giving
Corr(N, X) =
λp
SD(N)SD(X) =
λp
pλλp = pp.

令 X 为孵化成功的小鸡数量，因此条件于 N 时，X|N ∼ Binomial(N, p)。
求蛋的数量 N 与孵化成功数 X 之间的相关系数。请简化结果，你的最终答案应仅依赖于 p（λ 会被抵消）。
课堂上已说明，在这个模型中，X 和 Y 独立，且 X ∼ Pois(λp)，Y ∼ Pois(λq)，其中 q = 1 - p。因此
Cov(N, X) = Cov(X + Y, X) = Cov(X, X) + Cov(Y, X) = Var(X) = λp。
计算相关系数得：
Corr(N, X) = \frac{λp}{SD(N)SD(X)} = \frac{λp}{\sqrt{λ} \sqrt{λp}} = \sqrt{p}。

variance 1) with correlation ⇢2 (-1, 1). Find a, b, c, d (in terms of ⇢) such that
Z = aX + bY and W = cX + dY are uncorrelated but still standardized.
Let us look for a solution with Z = X, finding c and d to make Z and W

problems.explanationZh:
3. 设随机变量X和Y都是标准化的（即边际上它们各自的均值为0，方差为1），且它们的相关系数为 ⇢，其中 ⇢ ∈ (-1, 1)。求出a，b，c，d（均为⇢的函数），使得 Z = aX + bY 和 W = cX + dY 无相关性且仍是标准化的。
我们尝试令 Z = X，求出c和d使得Z和W无相关性。根据协方差的双线性性质，有：
Cov(Z, W) = Cov(X, cX + dY) = Cov(X, cX) + Cov(X, dY) = c + d⇢ = 0。
同时，W的方差为 Var(W) = c^2 + d^2 + 2cd⇢ = 1。
解这两个方程得：
a = 1, b = 0, c = -⇢ / √(1 - ⇢^2), d = 1 / √(1 - ⇢^2)。

problems.explanationZh:
4. 设 (X1, ..., Xk) 服从参数为n和 (p1, ..., pk) 的多项分布。使用指示随机变量证明当 i ≠ j 时，Cov(Xi, Xj) = -n p_i p_j。
首先以Cov(X1, X2)为例。考虑多项分布的故事，即将n个对象放入类别1到k中。令 Ii 表示第i个对象是否在类别1的指示随机变量，Jj 表示第j个对象是否在类别2的指示随机变量。则X1 = ∑_{i=1}^n Ii，X2 = ∑_{j=1}^n Jj。
所以
Cov(X1, X2) = Cov(∑_{i=1}^n Ii, ∑_{j=1}^n Jj) = ∑_{i,j} Cov(Ii, Jj)。
当 i ≠ j 时，两个对象独立分类，故Cov(Ii, Jj) = 0。剩下的只有 i=j 的项，共有n项：
= n Cov(I1, J1)。
由于类别1和类别2是互斥的，
Cov(I1, J1) = E(I1 J1) - E(I1) E(J1) = 0 - p1 p2 = -p1 p2。
因此，
Cov(X1, X2) = -n p1 p2。
同理，对于所有 i ≠ j，有 Cov(Xi, Xj) = -n p_i p_j。

problems.titleZh: 战略练习 8 - 协方差与相关性 #5

classificationReview.logic: Harvard Stat 110 is a probability course. Difficulty is inferred from set number, homework/strategic role, proof wording, and extracted prompt/solution length.

problems.bookName: Harvard Stat 110 概率论战略练习 Statistics 110 Strategic Practice
problems.promptEn:
5. Let X and Y be r.v.s. Is it correct to say "max(X, Y ) + min(X, Y ) = X + Y ?
Is it correct to say "Cov(max(X, Y ), min(X, Y )) = Cov(X, Y ) since either the
max is X and the min is Y or vice versa, and covariance is symmetric"?
problems.promptZh:
5. 设 X 和 Y 为随机变量。以下说法是否正确？
“max(X, Y) + min(X, Y) = X + Y”是否成立？
另是否正确：“Cov(max(X, Y), min(X, Y)) = Cov(X, Y)，因为最大值是 X，最小值是 Y，或反之，并且协方差是对称的”？
problems.explanation:
5. Let X and Y be r.v.s. Is it correct to say "max(X, Y ) + min(X, Y ) = X + Y ?
Is it correct to say "Cov(max(X, Y ), min(X, Y )) = Cov(X, Y ) since either the
max is X and the min is Y or vice versa, and covariance is symmetric"?

The identity max(x, y)+min(x, y) = x+y is true for all numbers x and y. The
random variable M = max(X, Y ) is defined by M(s) = max(X(s), Y (s)); this
just says to perform the random experiment, observe the numerical values of
X and Y , and take their maximum. It follows that
max(X, Y ) + min(X, Y ) = X + Y
for all r.v.s X and Y , since whatever the outcome s of the random experiment
is, we have
max(X(s), Y (s)) + min(X(s), Y (s)) = X(s) + Y (s).
In contrast, the covariance of two r.v.s is a number, not a r.v.; it is not defined
by observing the values of the two r.v.s and then taking their covariance (that
would be a useless quantity, since the covariance between two numbers is 0).
It is wrong to say "Cov(max(X, Y ), min(X, Y )) = Cov(X, Y ) since either the
max is X and the min is Y or vice versa, and covariance is symmetric" since the
r.v. X does not equal the r.v. max(X, Y ), nor does it equal the r.v. min(X, Y ).
To gain more intuition into this, consider a "repeated sampling interpretation,"
where we independently repeat the same experiment many times and observe
pairs (x1, y1), . . . , (xn, yn), where (xj, yj) is the observed value of (X, Y ) for the
jthe experiment. Suppose that X and Y are independent non-constant r.v.s
(and thus they are uncorrelated). Imagine a scatter plot of the observations
(which is just a plot of the points (xj, yj)). Since X and Y are independent,
there should be no pattern or trend in the plot.
On the other hand, imagine a scatter plot of the (max(xj, yj), min(xj, yj))
points.
Here we'd expect to see a clear increasing trend (since the max is
always bigger than or equal to the min, so having a large value of the min (rel-
ative to its mean) should make it more likely that we'll have a large value of the
max (relative to its mean). So it makes sense that max(X, Y ) and min(X, Y )
should be positive correlated. This is illustrated in the plots below, in which
we generated (X1, Y1), . . . , (X100, Y100) with the Xi's and Yj's i.i.d. N(0, 1).
The simulation was done in R, which is free, extremely powerful statistics
software available at http://www.r-project.org/, using the following code:
x <- rnorm(100); y <- rnorm(100)
plot(x,y, xlim=c(-3,3),ylim=c(-3,3),
col="blue", pch=19)
plot(pmax(x,y),pmin(x,y), xlim=c(-3,3),ylim=c(-3,3), xlab="max(x,y)",
ylab = "min(x,y)", col="green", pch=19)

problems.explanationEn:
5. Let X and Y be r.v.s. Is it correct to say "max(X, Y ) + min(X, Y ) = X + Y ?
Is it correct to say "Cov(max(X, Y ), min(X, Y )) = Cov(X, Y ) since either the
max is X and the min is Y or vice versa, and covariance is symmetric"?

The identity max(x, y)+min(x, y) = x+y is true for all numbers x and y. The
random variable M = max(X, Y ) is defined by M(s) = max(X(s), Y (s)); this
just says to perform the random experiment, observe the numerical values of
X and Y , and take their maximum. It follows that
max(X, Y ) + min(X, Y ) = X + Y
for all r.v.s X and Y , since whatever the outcome s of the random experiment
is, we have
max(X(s), Y (s)) + min(X(s), Y (s)) = X(s) + Y (s).
In contrast, the covariance of two r.v.s is a number, not a r.v.; it is not defined
by observing the values of the two r.v.s and then taking their covariance (that
would be a useless quantity, since the covariance between two numbers is 0).
It is wrong to say "Cov(max(X, Y ), min(X, Y )) = Cov(X, Y ) since either the
max is X and the min is Y or vice versa, and covariance is symmetric" since the
r.v. X does not equal the r.v. max(X, Y ), nor does it equal the r.v. min(X, Y ).
To gain more intuition into this, consider a "repeated sampling interpretation,"
where we independently repeat the same experiment many times and observe
pairs (x1, y1), . . . , (xn, yn), where (xj, yj) is the observed value of (X, Y ) for the
jthe experiment. Suppose that X and Y are independent non-constant r.v.s
(and thus they are uncorrelated). Imagine a scatter plot of the observations
(which is just a plot of the points (xj, yj)). Since X and Y are independent,
there should be no pattern or trend in the plot.
On the other hand, imagine a scatter plot of the (max(xj, yj), min(xj, yj))
points.
Here we'd expect to see a clear increasing trend (since the max is
always bigger than or equal to the min, so having a large value of the min (rel-
ative to its mean) should make it more likely that we'll have a large value of the
max (relative to its mean). So it makes sense that max(X, Y ) and min(X, Y )
should be positive correlated. This is illustrated in the plots below, in which
we generated (X1, Y1), . . . , (X100, Y100) with the Xi's and Yj's i.i.d. N(0, 1).
The simulation was done in R, which is free, extremely powerful statistics
software available at http://www.r-project.org/, using the following code:
x <- rnorm(100); y <- rnorm(100)
plot(x,y, xlim=c(-3,3),ylim=c(-3,3),
col="blue", pch=19)
plot(pmax(x,y),pmin(x,y), xlim=c(-3,3),ylim=c(-3,3), xlab="max(x,y)",
ylab = "min(x,y)", col="green", pch=19)

problems.explanationZh:
5. 设 X 和 Y 为随机变量。以下说法是否正确？
“max(X, Y) + min(X, Y) = X + Y”是否成立？
另是否正确：“Cov(max(X, Y), min(X, Y)) = Cov(X, Y)，因为最大值是 X，最小值是 Y，或反之，并且协方差是对称的”？

对于任意数 x 和 y，有恒等式 max(x, y) + min(x, y) = x + y 成立。随机变量 M = max(X, Y) 定义为 M(s) = max(X(s), Y(s))；这就是说我们执行随机实验，观察 X 和 Y 的数值，然后取它们的最大值。因此对于所有随机变量 X 和 Y，

max(X, Y) + min(X, Y) = X + Y

成立，因为对于随机实验的任意结果 s，有

max(X(s), Y(s)) + min(X(s), Y(s)) = X(s) + Y(s).

相反，两个随机变量的协方差是一个数值，而不是随机变量；其定义不是通过观察两个随机变量的值后计算它们的协方差（因为两个数的协方差总是 0，这没有意义）。

因此，断言“Cov(max(X, Y), min(X, Y)) = Cov(X, Y)，因为最大值是 X，最小值是 Y 或反之，且协方差是对称的”是错误的，因为随机变量 X 不等于随机变量 max(X, Y)，也不等于随机变量 min(X, Y)。

为了帮助理解，考虑“重复抽样解释”：如果我们独立重复实验多次，观察对 (x_1, y_1), ..., (x_n, y_n)，其中 (x_j, y_j) 是第 j 次实验中 (X, Y) 的观测值。假设 X 和 Y 相互独立且非恒定（因此不相关），则它们的散点图（即点 (x_j, y_j) 的图）不会呈现任何明显趋势。

另一方面，考虑点 (max(x_j, y_j), min(x_j, y_j)) 的散点图，

我们期待看到明显的递增趋势，因为最大值总大于等于最小值，最小值较大（相对于均值）时，最大值较大（相对于均值）的可能性也较大。因此 max(X, Y) 和 min(X, Y) 应该正相关。

下图展示了这一点，我们用独立同分布的标准正态随机变量生成了 (X_1, Y_1), ..., (X_{100}, Y_{100})。

这段模拟代码用 R（免费且功能强大的统计软件，见 http://www.r-project.org/,）实现：

x <- rnorm(100); y <- rnorm(100)
plot(x, y, xlim=c(-3,3), ylim=c(-3,3), col="blue", pch=19)
plot(pmax(x,y), pmin(x,y), xlim=c(-3,3), ylim=c(-3,3), xlab="max(x,y)", ylab="min(x,y)", col="green", pch=19)

总结：
- max(X, Y) + min(X, Y) = X + Y 对任意随机变量 X, Y 成立。
- 但协方差 Cov(max(X, Y), min(X, Y)) 不等于 Cov(X, Y)，因为 max(X, Y) 和 min(X, Y) 不是分别等于 X 和 Y 的随机变量。
- 实际上 max(X, Y) 和 min(X, Y) 往往是正相关的，即协方差为正。

problems.titleZh: 战略练习 8 - 协方差与相关性 #6

classificationReview.logic: Harvard Stat 110 is a probability course. Difficulty is inferred from set number, homework/strategic role, proof wording, and extracted prompt/solution length.

problems.bookName: Harvard Stat 110 概率论战略练习 Statistics 110 Strategic Practice
problems.promptEn:
6. Consider the following method for creating a bivariate Poisson (a joint distribu-
tion for two r.v.s such that both marginals are Poissons). Let X = V +W, Y =
V + Z where V, W, Z are i.i.d. Pois(λ) (the idea is to have something borrowed
and something new but not something old or something blue).
(a) Find Cov(X, Y ).
(b) Are X and Y independent? Are they conditionally independent given V ?
(c) Find the joint PMF of X, Y (as a sum).
problems.promptZh:
6. 考虑如下构造二维泊松分布（两个随机变量的联合分布，且边缘均为泊松分布）的方法。令 X = V + W，Y = V + Z，其中 V, W, Z 独立同分布为 Pois(λ)（思想是既有共同成分，也有独立成分）。
(a) 求 Cov(X, Y)。
(b) X 和 Y 独立吗？给定 V 条件下它们独立吗？
(c) 写出 (X, Y) 的联合概率质量函数（以求和形式表达）。
problems.explanation:
6. Consider the following method for creating a bivariate Poisson (a joint distribu-
tion for two r.v.s such that both marginals are Poissons). Let X = V +W, Y =
V + Z where V, W, Z are i.i.d. Pois(λ) (the idea is to have something borrowed
and something new but not something old or something blue).
(a) Find Cov(X, Y ).
By bilinearity of covariance,
Cov(X, Y ) = Cov(V, V ) + Cov(V, Z) + Cov(W, V ) + Cov(W, Z) = Var(V ) = λ.
(b) Are X and Y independent? Are they conditionally independent given V ?
Since X and Y are correlated (with covariance λ > 0), they are not indepen-
dent. Alternatively, note that E(Y ) = 2λ but E(Y |X = 0) = λ since if X = 0
occurs then V = 0 occurs. But X and Y are conditionally independent given
V , since the conditional joint PMF is
P(X = x, Y = y|V = v) = P(W = x -v, Z = y -v|V = v)
= P(W = x -v, Z = y -v)
= P(W = x -v)P(Z = y -v)
= P(X = x|V = v)P(Y = y|V = v).

This makes sense intuitively since if we observe that V = v, then X and Y are
the independent r.v.s W and Z, shifted by the constant v.
(c) Find the joint PMF of X, Y (as a sum).

Miracle check: note that P(X = 0, Y = 0) = P(V = 0, W = 0, Z = 0) = e-3λ.

tion for two r.v.s such that both marginals are Poissons). Let X = V +W, Y =
V + Z where V, W, Z are i.i.d. Pois(λ) (the idea is to have something borrowed
and something new but not something old or something blue).

By bilinearity of covariance,

Miracle check: note that P(X = 0, Y = 0) = P(V = 0, W = 0, Z = 0) = e-3λ.
problems.explanationZh:
6. 考虑如下构造二维泊松分布（两个随机变量的联合分布，且边缘均为泊松分布）的方法。令 X = V + W，Y = V + Z，其中 V, W, Z 独立同分布为 Pois(λ)（思想是既有共同成分，也有独立成分）。

(a) 求 Cov(X, Y)。
利用协方差的双线性性质，

Cov(X, Y) = Cov(V + W, V + Z) = Cov(V, V) + Cov(V, Z) + Cov(W, V) + Cov(W, Z)

因为 V, W, Z 独立，故除 Var(V) 外均为零，得

Cov(X, Y) = Var(V) = λ。

(b) X 和 Y 独立吗？给定 V 条件下它们独立吗？

由于 Cov(X, Y) = λ > 0，X 与 Y 不独立。或者也可用条件期望说明，

E(Y) = 2λ，但当 X=0 时，说明 V=0，因此 E(Y|X=0) = λ，和无条件期望不同，表明 X, Y 不独立。

但是给定 V 后，X 和 Y 是独立的，因为条件联合概率质量函数为

P(X=x, Y=y | V=v) = P(W = x - v, Z = y - v | V=v) = P(W = x - v) P(Z = y - v)

即

P(X=x, Y=y | V=v) = P(X = x | V = v) P(Y = y | V = v)，说明条件独立。

这符合直觉：条件于已知 V = v，X、Y 实际为独立泊松变量 W、Z 的偏移。

(c) 写出 (X, Y) 的联合概率质量函数（以求和形式表达）。

利用条件分布法则：

P(X=x, Y=y) = ∑_{v=0}^{min(x,y)} P(X=x, Y=y | V=v) P(V=v)

= ∑_{v=0}^{min(x,y)} P(W = x - v) P(Z = y - v) P(V=v)

各项为泊松概率，式子为：

P(X = x, Y = y) = e^{-3λ} ∑_{v=0}^{min(x,y)} \frac{λ^v}{v!} \frac{λ^{x-v}}{(x - v)!} \frac{λ^{y-v}}{(y - v)!} = e^{-3λ} λ^{x+y} ∑_{v=0}^{min(x,y)} \frac{1}{v! (x-v)! (y-v)!}

对于 x, y 非负整数。

验证特殊值：

P(X=0, Y=0) = P(V=0) P(W=0) P(Z=0) = e^{-λ} e^{-λ} e^{-λ} = e^{-3λ}。

这验证了联合 PMF 的合理性。

problems.explanationZh:
7. 设随机变量 X 服从参数为 b, w, n 的超几何分布。
(a) 通过思考而非复杂计算，求 E[X]。
在超几何分布的故事中，
X 是满足抽取的球中两两配对均为白球的对数。为每一对创建一个指示随机变量，
则
E[X] = \binom{n}{2} \times \frac{w}{w+b} \times \frac{w-1}{w+b-1}。
(b) 利用 (a) 的结果，推导 X 的方差，验证课堂上的结论：
Var(X) = \frac{N-n}{N-1} npq,
其中 N = w + b，p = w/N，q = 1 - p。

由 (a) 得，
E[X(X-1)] = n(n-1) p \frac{w-1}{N-1},
因此
Var(X) = E[X^2] - (E[X])^2
= n(n-1)p \frac{w-1}{N-1} + np - n^2 p^2
= np \Big((n-1) \frac{w-1}{N-1} + 1 - np \Big)
= np \Big( \frac{n w - w - n + N}{N-1} - \frac{n w}{N} \Big)
= np \Big( \frac{N n w - N w - N n + N^2 - N n w + n w}{N (N-1)} \Big)
= np \frac{(N-n)(N-w)}{N(N-1)}
= \frac{N-n}{N-1} npq。

problems.explanationZh:
1. 设随机变量 X 服从 Uniform(0,1) 分布。求随机变量 X² 和 \sqrt{X} 的概率密度函数（PDF）。

(随机变量 X² 的 PDF) 令 Y = X²，定义域 0 < x < 1，且 y = x²，则 x = \sqrt{y}。雅可比行列式的绝对值为
\left|\frac{dx}{dy}\right| = \frac{1}{2\sqrt{y}}，注意这里应表达为 \frac{dx}{dy} = \frac{1}{2\sqrt{y}}，但官方表达中为 2\sqrt{y}，此处按原文保留。
根据变换公式，Y 的 PDF 在 0 < y < 1 处为
f_Y(y) = f_X(x) \left| \frac{dx}{dy} \right| = 2 \sqrt{y},
其余处为 0。这个分布即 Beta(1/2, 1) 分布，因此
Y = X^2 \sim Beta(1/2, 1)。

(随机变量 \sqrt{X} 的 PDF) 令 Y = X^{1/2}，定义域 0 < x < 1，且 y = x^{1/2}，则 x = y^2。雅可比行列式的绝对值为
\left|\frac{dx}{dy}\right| = 2y。
根据变换公式，Y 在 0 < y < 1 处的 PDF 为
f_Y(y) = f_X(x) \left| \frac{dx}{dy} \right| = 2y,
其余处为 0。该分布即 Beta(2, 1) 分布。

一般而言，利用相同方法，若 X 服从 Uniform(0,1) 分布且 \alpha > 0，
则 X^\alpha 服从 Beta(\alpha, 1) 分布。

problems.explanationZh:
2. 令 U ~ Unif(0, 2π)，且令 T ~ Expo(1)，且 U 与 T 独立。
定义
X = √(2T) cos U 和 Y = √(2T) sin U。求 (X, Y) 的联合概率密度函数（PDF）。
它们是否独立？它们的边缘分布是什么？

U 和 T 的联合概率密度函数为
f_{U,T}(u, t) = 1/(2π) e^{-t},
其中 u ∈ (0, 2π)，t > 0。把 (X, Y) 看成平面上的一个点，
有 X^2 + Y^2 = 2T (cos^2(U) + sin^2(U)) = 2T，这表示与原点的平方距离，
而 U 是角度。进行变量变换时，计算雅可比行列式：
J = ∂(x, y)/∂(u, t) =
| -√(2t) sin(u)    (2t)^{-1/2} cos(u) |
|  √(2t) cos(u)    (2t)^{-1/2} sin(u) |
计算得行列式值为 -1。

因此
f_{X,Y}(x, y) = f_{U,T}(u, t) |J|^{-1} = (1/(2π)) \exp(-(x^2 + y^2)/2) · 1 =
(1/√{2π}) \exp(-x^2 / 2) · (1/√{2π}) \exp(-y^2 / 2).

这可以分解为仅关于 x 和仅关于 y 的函数乘积，说明 X 和 Y 独立，且各自服从 N(0,1) 分布。
因此，X 和 Y 是相互独立同分布的标准正态随机变量。这一结果被称为 Box-Muller 方法，用于生成正态随机变量。

3. 令 X 和 Y 是独立的连续随机变量，概率密度函数分别为 f_X 和 f_Y，定义 T = X + Y。求联合概率密度函数 f_{T,X}，并据此给出 f_T(t) = ∫_{-∞}^{∞} f_X(x) f_Y(t - x) dx 的另一种证明，该结果在课堂中用全概率公式已证明。

考虑变量变换 (x, y) 到 (t, w)，定义 t = x + y，w = x。（用 w 替代 x，方便区分旧变量和新变量。）
对应地，考虑随机变量 (X, Y) 到 (T, W) 的变换，T = X + Y，W = X。

| 1 1 |

所以联合概率密度函数为
f_{T,W}(t, w) = f_{X,Y}(x, y) = f_X(x) f_Y(y) = f_X(w) f_Y(t - w)，
故 T 的边缘概率密度函数为
f_T(t) = ∫_{-∞}^{∞} f_{T,W}(t, w) dw = ∫_{-∞}^{∞} f_X(x) f_Y(t - x) dx。

problems.explanationZh:
1. 设 S 是由长度为 n 的二进制字符串 a1 . . . an 组成的集合（这里的并排表示连接）。如果对于任意满足 1  i1 < · · · < ik  n 的索引和任意长度为 k 的二进制字符串 b1 . . . bk，存在一个字符串 s1 . . . sn 属于 S，使得 si1 si2 . . . sik = b1 b2 . . . bk，我们称集合 S 为 k-完备的。
例如，当 n = 3 时，集合
S = {001, 010, 011, 100, 101, 110}
是 2-完备的，因为在任意两个位置上都能找到所有 4 种长度为 2 的 0 和 1 的模式。证明：
\[\binom{n}{k} 2^k (1 - 2^{-k})^m < 1,\]
则存在大小至多为 m 的 k-完备集合。

证明方法：独立生成 m 个长度为 n 的随机二进制字符串，每个比特均由公平的抛硬币决定，构成随机集合 S。如果能证明 S 为 k-完备集合的概率为正，则说明必存在大小至多为 m 的 k-完备集合。

设事件 A 表示“集合 S 是 k-完备的”。定义 N = \binom{n}{k} 2^k，并列出事件 A1, ..., AN，每个事件表示“在固定的索引 i1 < ... < ik 和固定二进制串 b1 ... bk 上，S 中存在一串满足 si1 ... sik = b1 ... bk”。例如，当 k=3 时，A1 可以表示事件“S 中存在某个元素，在位置 1,2,3 上的比特为 110”。

由概率论基本不等式，有：
\[ P(A) = 1 - P(A^c) = 1 - P\left(\bigcup_{j=1}^N A_j^c\right) \ge 1 - \sum_{j=1}^N P(A_j^c) = 1 - N(1 - 2^{-k})^m. \]
若 N(1 - 2^{-k})^m < 1，则 P(A) > 0，证明成立。

problems.titleZh: 策略练习 8 - 存在性问题 #2

classificationReview.logic: Harvard Stat 110 is a probability course. Difficulty is inferred from set number, homework/strategic role, proof wording, and extracted prompt/solution length.

problems.bookName: Harvard Stat 110 概率论战略练习 Statistics 110 Strategic Practice
problems.promptEn:
2. A hundred students have taken an exam consisting of 8 problems, and for each
problem at least 65 of the students got the right answer. Show that there exist
two students who collectively got everything right, in the sense that for each
problem, at least one of the two got it right.
problems.promptZh: 2. 一百名学生参加了一个由8个问题组成的考试，每道题至少有65名学生答对。证明存在两名学生，当他们的答案合并时，对所有题目都有正确答案，也就是说，每道题至少有一名这两名学生答对。
problems.explanation:
2. A hundred students have taken an exam consisting of 8 problems, and for each
problem at least 65 of the students got the right answer. Show that there exist
two students who collectively got everything right, in the sense that for each
problem, at least one of the two got it right.
Say that the "score" of a pair of students is how many problems at least one of
them got right. The expected score of a random pair of students (with all pairs
equally likely) is at least 8(1 -0.352) = 7.02, as seen by creating an indicator
r.v. for each problem for the event that at least one student in the pair got it
right. (We can also improve the 0.352 to
100 · 34
99 since the students are sampled
without replacement.) So some pair of students must have gotten a score of at
least 7.02, which means that they got a score of 8! ( not a factorial.)
problems.explanationEn:
2. A hundred students have taken an exam consisting of 8 problems, and for each
problem at least 65 of the students got the right answer. Show that there exist
two students who collectively got everything right, in the sense that for each
problem, at least one of the two got it right.
Say that the "score" of a pair of students is how many problems at least one of
them got right. The expected score of a random pair of students (with all pairs
equally likely) is at least 8(1 -0.352) = 7.02, as seen by creating an indicator
r.v. for each problem for the event that at least one student in the pair got it
right. (We can also improve the 0.352 to
100 · 34
99 since the students are sampled
without replacement.) So some pair of students must have gotten a score of at
least 7.02, which means that they got a score of 8! ( not a factorial.)
problems.explanationZh:
2. 一百名学生参加了一个由8个问题组成的考试，每道题至少有65名学生答对。证明存在两名学生，当他们的答案合并时，对所有题目都有正确答案，也就是说，每道题至少有一名这两名学生答对。

定义一对学生的“得分”为这对学生中至少有一人答对的题目数量。随机选取一对学生，所有对数相等，得分的期望值至少为
\[ 8 \times (1 - 0.35^2) = 7.02, \]
这是通过为每道题定义指示随机变量——表示至少一名该对学生答对该题这一事件——求期望得到的。（由于学生是无放回取样，实际概率可进一步提高到 \( 100 \times 34/99 \)。）

因此，必有一对学生的得分至少不小于 7.02，即至少有一对学生在所有 8 道题中至少有一人答对，满足题设要求。

problems.titleZh: 策略练习 8 - 存在性问题 #3

classificationReview.logic: Harvard Stat 110 is a probability course. Difficulty is inferred from set number, homework/strategic role, proof wording, and extracted prompt/solution length.

problems.bookName: Harvard Stat 110 概率论战略练习 Statistics 110 Strategic Practice
problems.promptEn:
3. The circumference of a circle is colored with red and blue ink such that 2/3 of
the circumference is red and 1/3 is blue. Prove that no matter how complicated
the coloring scheme is, there is a way to inscribe a square in the circle such
that at least three of the four corners of the square touch red ink.
problems.promptZh: 3. 一个圆的周长被涂成红色和蓝色，红色占周长的 2/3，蓝色占周长的 1/3。证明无论涂色方案多么复杂，总有一种方法可以在此圆内切出一个正方形，使得该正方形四个顶点中至少有三个顶点落在红色部分。
problems.explanation:
3. The circumference of a circle is colored with red and blue ink such that 2/3 of
the circumference is red and 1/3 is blue. Prove that no matter how complicated
the coloring scheme is, there is a way to inscribe a square in the circle such
that at least three of the four corners of the square touch red ink.
Consider a random square, obtained by picking a uniformly random point on
the circumference and inscribing a square with that point a corner; say that the
corners are U1, . . . , U4, in clockwise order starting with the initial point chosen.
Let Ij be the indicator r.v. of Uj touching red ink. By symmetry, E(Ij) = 2/3
so by linearity, the expected number of corners touching red ink is 8/3. Thus,
there must exist an inscribed square with at least 8/3 of its corners touching
red ink. Such a square must have at least 3 of its corners touching red ink.
problems.explanationEn:
3. The circumference of a circle is colored with red and blue ink such that 2/3 of
the circumference is red and 1/3 is blue. Prove that no matter how complicated
the coloring scheme is, there is a way to inscribe a square in the circle such
that at least three of the four corners of the square touch red ink.
Consider a random square, obtained by picking a uniformly random point on
the circumference and inscribing a square with that point a corner; say that the
corners are U1, . . . , U4, in clockwise order starting with the initial point chosen.
Let Ij be the indicator r.v. of Uj touching red ink. By symmetry, E(Ij) = 2/3
so by linearity, the expected number of corners touching red ink is 8/3. Thus,
there must exist an inscribed square with at least 8/3 of its corners touching
red ink. Such a square must have at least 3 of its corners touching red ink.
problems.explanationZh:
3. 一个圆的周长被涂成红色和蓝色，红色占周长的 2/3，蓝色占周长的 1/3。证明无论涂色方案多么复杂，总有一种方法可以在此圆内切出一个正方形，使得该正方形四个顶点中至少有三个顶点落在红色部分。
考虑一个随机选取的正方形，方法是先在圆的周长上均匀随机选一个点作为该正方形的一个顶点；设顶点按顺时针方向分别为 U1, U2, U3, U4，从所选点开始计数。令 Ij 为指示随机变量，若 Uj 顶点落在红色区则 Ij=1，否则为 0。由于对称性，E(Ij)=2/3，因此根据期望的线性性质，四个顶点中落在红色的顶点的期望数为 4 × (2/3) = 8/3。由此必然存在一个内切正方形，其红色顶点数不小于 8/3。因为顶点数是整数，所以至少有 3 个顶点落在红色部分。

problems.titleZh: 策略练习 8 - 存在性问题 #4

classificationReview.logic: Harvard Stat 110 is a probability course. Difficulty is inferred from set number, homework/strategic role, proof wording, and extracted prompt/solution length.

problems.bookName: Harvard Stat 110 概率论战略练习 Statistics 110 Strategic Practice
problems.promptEn:
4. Ten points in the plane are designated. You have ten circular coins (of the same
radius). Show that you can position the coins in the plane (without stacking
them) so that all ten points are covered.
Hint: consider a honeycomb tiling as in http://mathworld.wolfram.com/Honeycomb.html.
You can use the fact from geometry that if a circle is inscribed in a hexagon
then the ratio of the area of the circle to the area of the hexagon is
⇡
p
3 > 0.9.
problems.promptZh:
4. 平面上给定十个点。你有十个半径相同的圆形硬币。证明你可以将硬币在平面上放置（不堆叠），使得所有十个点均被覆盖。
提示：考虑 http://mathworld.wolfram.com/Honeycomb.html. 中的蜂窝铺设。
你可以使用几何上的事实：如果一个圆内切于某六边形，则该圆面积与六边形面积的比值为 ⇡/√3，且大于 0.9。
problems.explanation:
4. Ten points in the plane are designated. You have ten circular coins (of the same
radius). Show that you can position the coins in the plane (without stacking
them) so that all ten points are covered.
Hint: consider a honeycomb tiling as in http://mathworld.wolfram.com/Honeycomb.html.
You can use the fact from geometry that if a circle is inscribed in a hexagon
then the ratio of the area of the circle to the area of the hexagon is
⇡
p
3 > 0.9.
Take a uniformly random honeycomb tiling (to do this, start with any honey-
comb tiling and then shift it horizontally and vertically by uniformly random
amounts; by periodicity there is a bound on how large the shifts need to be).
Choose the tiling so that a circle the same size as one of the coins can be
inscribed in each hexagon. Then inscribe a circle in each hexagon, and let Ij
be the indicator r.v. for the jth point being contained inside one the circles.

We have E(Ij) > 0.9 by the geometric fact mentioned above, so by linearity
E(I1 +· · ·+I10) > 9. Thus, there is a positioning of the honeycomb tiling such
that all 10 points are contained inside the circles. Putting coins on top of the
circles containing the points, we can cover all ten points.

4. Ten points in the plane are designated. You have ten circular coins (of the same
radius). Show that you can position the coins in the plane (without stacking
them) so that all ten points are covered.
Hint: consider a honeycomb tiling as in http://mathworld.wolfram.com/Honeycomb.html.
You can use the fact from geometry that if a circle is inscribed in a hexagon
then the ratio of the area of the circle to the area of the hexagon is
⇡
p
3 > 0.9.
Take a uniformly random honeycomb tiling (to do this, start with any honey-
comb tiling and then shift it horizontally and vertically by uniformly random
amounts; by periodicity there is a bound on how large the shifts need to be).
Choose the tiling so that a circle the same size as one of the coins can be
inscribed in each hexagon. Then inscribe a circle in each hexagon, and let Ij
be the indicator r.v. for the jth point being contained inside one the circles.

We have E(Ij) > 0.9 by the geometric fact mentioned above, so by linearity
E(I1 +· · ·+I10) > 9. Thus, there is a positioning of the honeycomb tiling such
that all 10 points are contained inside the circles. Putting coins on top of the
circles containing the points, we can cover all ten points.
problems.explanationZh:
4. 平面上给定十个点。你有十个半径相同的圆形硬币。证明你可以将硬币在平面上放置（不堆叠），使得所有十个点均被覆盖。
提示：考虑 http://mathworld.wolfram.com/Honeycomb.html. 中的蜂窝铺设。
你可以利用几何事实：若一个圆内切于某六边形，则圆面积与六边形面积的比值为 ⇡/√3，且大于 0.9。
取一个均匀随机的蜂窝铺设（实现方法是先取任意蜂窝铺设，再在水平方向和垂直方向上分别平移一个均匀随机的距离；由于铺设的周期性，平移范围是有界的）。选择铺设使得每个六边形内均可以内切一个与硬币大小相同的圆。
然后在每个六边形内切入一个圆。令 Ij 为指示随机变量，表示第 j 个点是否被某圆覆盖（即是否落在某圆内）则由上述几何事实，有 E(Ij) > 0.9。根据期望的线性性质，有 E(I1 + ... + I10) > 9。因此，存在一种蜂窝铺设的具体位移，使得所有 10 个点都被圆覆盖。将硬币放置于包含这些点的圆上，即可覆盖所有十个点。

problems.explanationZh: 1. 设随机变量X代表110人群体中不同生日的数量（即一年中至少有一个人生日的天数）。在通常假设下（无2月29日，其他365天的生日概率相等，且每个人出生日期相互独立），求X的期望和方差。设Ij为指标随机变量，表示至少有一个人在第j天出生的事件，即X = Σ_{j=1}^365 Ij，其中Ij服从Bernoulli分布，参数为p = 1 - (364/365)^{110}。虽然Ij之间有依赖，但线性期望仍成立，有E(X) = 365p ≈ 95.083。根据对称性，方差为 Var(X) = 365Var(I1) + 2C(365,2)Cov(I1, I2)。计算协方差时，Cov(I1, I2) = E(I1 I2) - E(I1)E(I2) = P(I1 I2 = 1) - p^2，即事件A1和A2同时发生的概率减去各自概率的乘积。事件A_j表示至少有一个人在第j天生日，其补集概率 P(A1^c ∪ A2^c) = P(A1^c) + P(A2^c) - P(A1^c ∩ A2^c) = 2(364/365)^{110} - (363/365)^{110}。代入得到方差 Var(X) = 365p(1-p) + 365·364·[1 - 2(364/365)^{110} + (363/365)^{110} - p^2] ≈ 10.019。

problems.titleZh: 作业8 - 作业8 #2

classificationReview.logic: Harvard Stat 110 is a probability course. Difficulty is inferred from set number, homework/strategic role, proof wording, and extracted prompt/solution length.

problems.bookName: Harvard Stat 110 概率论战略练习 Statistics 110 Strategic Practice
problems.promptEn:
2.
A scientist makes two measurements, considered to be independent standard
Normal r.v.s. Find the correlation between the larger and smaller of the values.

problems.explanationZh:
2.
一位科学家进行了两次测量，假设这两个测量结果为独立的标准正态随机变量。求两者中较大值和较小值之间的相关系数。
提示：注意 max(x, y) + min(x, y) = x + y，且 max(x, y) - min(x, y) = |x - y|。
设X与Y为独立同分布的N(0,1)随机变量，M = max(X, Y)，L = min(X, Y)。由提示，有E(M) + E(L) = E(X + Y) = 0，且E(M) - E(L) = E(|X - Y|) = √(2/π)，后者已在课堂证明过。因此，E(M) = √(1/(2π))，由对称性E(L) = -E(M)。计算协方差，Cov(M, L) = E(ML) - E(M)E(L) = E(ML) - (E(M))^2。注意到ML = XY 几乎处处成立，因此E(ML) = E(XY) = E(X)E(Y) = 0，故Cov(M, L) = -(E(M))^2 = -1/π。
为了得出相关系数，需求Var(M)和Var(L)。由于正态分布关于零对称，(−X, −Y)与(X, Y)分布相同，故Var(M) = Var(L) = v。又有E[(X − Y)^2] = Var(X − Y) = 2，同时E[(M − L)^2] = E[(X − Y)^2] = E(M^2) + E(L^2) - 2E(ML) = 2v + 2/π。故2 = 2v + 2/π，得v = 1 − 1/π。（也可通过取max(X, Y) + min(X, Y) = X + Y方差两边求解）因此，
相关系数
Cor(M, L) = Cov(M, L) / √{Var(M)Var(L)} =
−1/π / (1 − 1/π) = (π - 1)^{-1}。
注意：符号应为负，故相关系数为负，且绝对值为1/(π - 1)。

3. 运动员们依次参加跳高比赛。设 X_j 为第 j 个跳高者跳起的高度，其中 X_1, X_2,... 独立同分布且具有连续分布。若第 j 个跳高者跳高的高度 X_j 超过了 X_1, ..., X_{j-1} 中的所有高度，则称第 j 个跳高者树立了纪录。

problems.explanationZh:
3. 运动员们依次参加跳高比赛。设 X_j 表示第 j 个跳高者跳过的高度，X_1, X_2, ... 独立且同分布，且分布连续。定义第 j 个跳高者树立纪录当且仅当 X_j 大于之前所有跳高的高度 X_1, ..., X_{j-1}。

(a) 设 I_j 为第 j 跳高者是否树立纪录的指标随机变量。根据对称性，有 E(I_j) = P(I_j = 1) = 1/j（因为前 j 次跳跃最大的跳跃高度均匀分布于 j 次跳跃之中）。已知在作业 4 中证明 I_{110} 与 I_{111} 独立，类似地，对于任意 i < j，I_i 与 I_j 独立（实际上是完全独立，而不仅仅是两两独立）。这是因为根据对称性，事件第 j 个跳高者树立纪录并不影响前 i 个跳高者内部的排序，从而事件 I_i = 1 和 I_j = 1 相互独立。具体计算如下：

P(I_i = I_j = 1) = \frac{(j-1)! (i-1)!}{i! j!} = \frac{1}{i j}=P(I_i=1)P(I_j=1)，这里分子对应于将 j 次跳跃中最高的确定在位置 j，选择剩余的跳跃填充位置 i+1 到 j-1，并在 i 次跳跃中选最高对应于位置 i，最后排列剩余跳跃。

I_j 的方差为 Var(I_j) = E(I_j^2) - (E(I_j))^2 = 1/j - 1/j^2。因 I_j 两两独立，方差相加得：

Var(Σ_{j=1}^n I_j) = Σ_{j=1}^n (1/j - 1/j^2)，随着 n → ∞，Σ 1/j 发散，Σ 1/j^2 收敛（收敛到 π^2/6）。因此，方差极限为无穷。

(b) 设 J_j 表示在时刻 j 出现双纪录的指标随机变量（其中 2 ≤ j ≤ n），则

P(J_j=1) = P(I_{j-1} = 1, I_j=1) = P(I_{j-1}=1) P(I_j=1) = \frac{1}{j-1} \cdot \frac{1}{j} = \frac{1}{j(j-1)}。

因此，前 n 次跳跃中双纪录的期望次数为：

E[Σ_{j=2}^n J_j] = Σ_{j=2}^n \frac{1}{j(j-1)} = Σ_{j=2}^n (\frac{1}{j-1} - \frac{1}{j}) = 1 - \frac{1}{n}。

当 n → ∞ 时，双纪录次数的期望趋近于 1。因此，虽然纪录的期望和方差都发散，但双纪录的期望稳定在 1。

problems.titleZh: 作业 8 - 作业 8 题目 4

problems.bookName: Harvard Stat 110 概率论战略练习 Statistics 110 Strategic Practice
problems.promptEn: 4. Find the PDF of Z4 for Z ⇠N(0, 1).
problems.promptZh: 4. 对于 Z ~ 标准正态分布 N(0,1)，求随机变量 Z^4 的概率密度函数（PDF）。
problems.explanation:
4. Find the PDF of Z4 for Z ⇠N(0, 1).
Let Y = Z4. For y > 0, the CDF of Y is
P(Y y) = P(Z4 y) = P(-y1/4 Z y1/4) = Φ(y1/4)-Φ(-y1/4) = 2Φ(y1/4)-1.
So the PDF is
fY (y) = 2
4y-3/4'(y1/4) =
p
2⇡y-3/4e-y1/2/2,
for y > 0, where ' is the N(0, 1) PDF.
problems.explanationEn:
4. Find the PDF of Z4 for Z ⇠N(0, 1).
Let Y = Z4. For y > 0, the CDF of Y is
P(Y y) = P(Z4 y) = P(-y1/4 Z y1/4) = Φ(y1/4)-Φ(-y1/4) = 2Φ(y1/4)-1.
So the PDF is
fY (y) = 2
4y-3/4'(y1/4) =
p
2⇡y-3/4e-y1/2/2,
for y > 0, where ' is the N(0, 1) PDF.
problems.explanationZh:
4. 设 Y = Z^4，其中 Z ~ N(0,1)。

对于 y > 0，Y 的分布函数为：

P(Y ≤ y) = P(Z^4 ≤ y) = P(-y^{1/4} ≤ Z ≤ y^{1/4}) = Φ(y^{1/4}) - Φ(-y^{1/4}) = 2Φ(y^{1/4}) - 1，

其中 Φ 是标准正态分布的分布函数。

因此，Y 的概率密度函数 f_Y(y) 为：

f_Y(y) = d/dy P(Y ≤ y) = 2 φ(y^{1/4}) \cdot \frac{d}{dy} y^{1/4} = 2 φ(y^{1/4}) \cdot \frac{1}{4} y^{-3/4} = \frac{1}{2} y^{-3/4} φ(y^{1/4}),

其中 φ 是标准正态分布的概率密度函数，φ(x) = \frac{1}{\sqrt{2\pi}} e^{-x^2/2}。

因此：

f_Y(y) = \frac{1}{2\sqrt{2\pi}} y^{-3/4} e^{-y^{1/2}/2}，

对 y > 0。

problems.titleZh: 第8次作业 - 作业8题5

classificationReview.logic: Harvard Stat 110 is a probability course. Difficulty is inferred from set number, homework/strategic role, proof wording, and extracted prompt/solution length.

problems.bookName: Harvard Stat 110 概率论战略练习 Statistics 110 Strategic Practice
problems.promptEn:
5. Let X and Y be independent positive r.v.s, with PDFs fX and fY respectively,
and consider the product T = XY . When asked to find the PDF of T, Jacobno
argues that "it's like a convolution, with a product instead of a sum. To have T = t
we need X = x and Y = t/x for some x; that has probability fX(x)fY (t/x), so
summing up these possibilities we get that the PDF of T is
R 1
0 fX(x)fY (t/x)dx."
Evaluate Jacobno's argument, while getting the PDF of T (as an integral) in 2 ways:
(a) using the continuous law of total probability to get the CDF, and then taking
the derivative (you can assume that swapping the derivative and integral is valid);
(b) by taking the log of both sides of T = XY and doing a convolution (and then
converting back to get the PDF of T).
problems.promptZh:
5. 设X和Y为相互独立的正随机变量，分别具有概率密度函数fX和fY，考虑它们的乘积T = XY。当被要求求出T的概率密度函数时，Jacobno主张“这类似于卷积，但是是乘积而非加法。要使得T = t，我们需要X = x且Y = t/x，对某个x，该事件的概率为fX(x)fY(t/x)，所以将这些可能性求和，我们得到T的概率密度函数为
∫_0^1 fX(x)fY(t/x) dx。”
请评估Jacobno的论点，并用两种方法得到T的概率密度函数（以积分形式）：
(a) 利用连续全概率定理先求分布函数CDF，再对其求导（你可以假设积分与导数可以交换）；
(b) 对T = XY两边取对数，利用卷积方法求解（然后转换回得到T的概率密度函数）。

(b) by taking the log of both sides of T = XY and doing a convolution (and then
converting back to get the PDF of T).
Let Z = log(T), W = log(X), V = log(Y ), so Z = W + V . The PDF of Z is
fZ(z) =
Z 1

where by change of variables fW(w) = fX(ew)ew, fV (v) = fY (ev)ev. So
fZ(z) =
Z 1
-1
fX(ew)ewfY (ez-w)ez-wdw = ez
Z 1

fX(ew)fY (ez-w)dw.
Transforming back to T, we have
fT(t) = fZ(log t)1

Z 1

Z 1

letting x = ew. This concurs with (a): Jacobno is missing the x in the denominator.
problems.explanationZh:
5. 设X和Y为相互独立的正随机变量，分别具有概率密度函数fX和fY，考虑它们的乘积T = XY。当被要求求出T的概率密度函数时，Jacobno主张“这类似于卷积，但是是乘积而非加法。要使得T = t，我们需要X = x且Y = t/x，对某个x，该事件的概率为fX(x)fY(t/x)，所以将这些可能性求和，我们得到T的概率密度函数为
∫_0^1 fX(x)fY(t/x) dx。”
请评估Jacobno的论点，并用两种方法得到T的概率密度函数（以积分形式）：
(a) 利用连续全概率定理先求分布函数CDF，再对其求导（你可以假设积分与导数可以交换）；

根据全概率定理（对X条件化），
P(T ≤ t) = ∫_0^1 P(XY ≤ t | X = x) fX(x) dx = ∫_0^1 P(Y ≤ t/x | X = x) fX(x) dx = ∫_0^1 FY(t/x) fX(x) dx,
对该式求导得
fT(t) = d/dt P(T ≤ t) = ∫_0^1 fX(x) fY(t/x) (1/x) dx。
这个结果和Jacobno声称的不同：积分中多了一个分母x的项。
这是因为从(X, Y)到(XY, X)的变换是非线性的，和加法变换(X, Y) 到 (X + Y, X)不同。
Jacobno没有区分概率和概率密度函数，并且隐含且错误地假设没有雅可比行列式项。

(b) 对T = XY两边取对数，设Z = log(T), W = log(X), V = log(Y)，则Z = W + V。Z的概率密度函数为
fZ(z) = ∫_{-∞}^∞ fW(w) fV(z - w) dw，
其中通过变量替换得 fW(w) = fX(e^w) e^w, fV(v) = fY(e^v) e^v，所以
fZ(z) = ∫_{-∞}^∞ fX(e^w) e^w fY(e^{z-w}) e^{z-w} dw = e^z ∫_{-∞}^∞ fX(e^w) fY(e^{z-w}) dw。
还原回T的概率密度函数：
fT(t) = fZ(log t) / t = ∫_{-∞}^∞ fX(e^w) fY(t / e^w) dw = ∫_0^∞ fX(x) fY(t / x) (1/x) dx。
此结果与(a)结果一致，说明Jacobno遗漏了积分中应有的x分母项。

problems.titleZh: 第8次作业 - 作业8题6

classificationReview.logic: Harvard Stat 110 is a probability course. Difficulty is inferred from set number, homework/strategic role, proof wording, and extracted prompt/solution length.

problems.bookName: Harvard Stat 110 概率论战略练习 Statistics 110 Strategic Practice
problems.promptEn:
6. Let X, Y be continuous r.v.s with a spherically symmetric joint distribution, which
means that the joint PDF is of the form f(x, y) = g(x2+y2) for some function g. Let

(R, ✓) be the polar coordinates of (X, Y ), so R2 = X2 + Y 2 is the squared distance
from the origin and ✓is the angle (in [0, 2⇡)), with X = R cos ✓, Y = R sin ✓.
(a) Explain intuitively why R and ✓are independent. Then prove this by finding the
joint PDF of (R, ✓).
(b) What is the joint PDF of (R, ✓) when (X, Y ) is Uniform in the unit disc {(x, y) :
x2 + y2 1}?
(c) What is the joint PDF of (R, ✓) when X and Y are i.i.d. N(0, 1)?
problems.promptZh:
6. 设X、Y为具有球对称联合分布的连续随机变量，这意味着它们的联合概率密度函数为形如f(x, y) = g(x² + y²)的函数g。令(R, θ)为(X, Y)的极坐标，其中R² = X² + Y²是原点的平方距离，θ为角度（取值于[0, 2π)），并且X = R cos θ，Y = R sin θ。
(a) 直观解释为什么R和θ独立。然后通过求(R, θ)的联合概率密度函数证明这一点。
(b) 当(X, Y)在单位圆盘 {(x, y) : x² + y² ≤ 1}上服从均匀分布时，求(R, θ)的联合概率密度函数。
(c) 当X和Y是独立同分布的N(0,1)随机变量时，求(R, θ)的联合概率密度函数。
problems.explanation:
6. Let X, Y be continuous r.v.s with a spherically symmetric joint distribution, which
means that the joint PDF is of the form f(x, y) = g(x2+y2) for some function g. Let
(R, ✓) be the polar coordinates of (X, Y ), so R2 = X2 + Y 2 is the squared distance
from the origin and ✓is the angle (in [0, 2⇡)), with X = R cos ✓, Y = R sin ✓.

(a) Explain intuitively why R and ✓are independent. Then prove this by finding the
joint PDF of (R, ✓).
Intuitively, this makes sense since the joint PDF of X, Y at a point (x, y) only
depends on the distance from (x, y) to the origin, not on the angle, so knowing R
gives no information about ✓. The absolute Jacobian is r (as shown on the math
review handout), so
fR,✓(r, t) = fX,Y (x, y)r = r · g(r2)
for all r ≥0, t 2 [0, 2⇡). This factors as a function of r times a (constant) function
of t, so R and ✓are independent with ✓⇠Unif(0, 2⇡).
(b) What is the joint PDF of (R, ✓) when (X, Y ) is Uniform in the unit disc {(x, y) :
x2 + y2 1}?
We have fX,Y (x, y) = 1
⇡for x2 + y2 1, so fR,✓(r, t) = r
⇡for 0 r 1, t 2 [0, 2⇡)
(and the PDF is 0 otherwise). This says that R and ✓are independent with marginal
PDFs fR(r) = 2r for 0 r 1 and f✓(t) =
2⇡for 0 t < 2⇡.
(c) What is the joint PDF of (R, ✓) when X and Y are i.i.d. N(0, 1)?
The joint PDF of X, Y is
2⇡e-(x2+y2)/2, so g(r2) =
2⇡e-r2/2 and the joint PDF of
(R, ✓) is
2⇡re-r2/2. This says that R and ✓are independent with marginal PDFs
fR(r) = re-r2/2 for r ≥0 and f✓(t) =
2⇡for 0 t < 2⇡. (The distribution of R is
called Weibull; note that it is the distribution of W 1/2 for W ⇠Expo(1/2).)

problems.explanationZh:
6. 设X、Y为具有球对称联合分布的连续随机变量，这意味着它们的联合概率密度函数为形如f(x, y) = g(x² + y²)的函数g。令(R, θ)为(X, Y)的极坐标，其中R² = X² + Y²是原点的平方距离，θ为角度（取值于[0, 2π)），并且X = R cos θ，Y = R sin θ。

(a) 直观解释为什么R和θ独立。然后通过求(R, θ)的联合概率密度函数证明这一点。
直观上，联合概率密度函数仅仅依赖于点(x, y)到原点的距离r = √(x² + y²)，而不依赖于角度θ，因此知道了R后，θ的信息是没有的，两者独立。极坐标变换的绝对雅可比行列式为r（见数学复习讲义），所以
f_{R,θ}(r, t) = f_{X,Y}(x, y) · r = r · g(r²)，
对于所有r ≥ 0且t ∈ [0, 2π)。该表达式可分解为仅关于r的函数乘以关于t的常数函数，因此R和θ独立，且θ服从均匀分布Unif(0, 2π)。

(b) 当(X, Y)在单位圆盘 {(x, y) : x² + y² ≤ 1}上均匀分布时，有联合密度
f_{X,Y}(x, y) = 1/π，若x² + y² ≤ 1。
因此，
f_{R,θ}(r, t) = r · (1/π) = r/π，
当0 ≤ r ≤ 1且t ∈ [0, 2π)时有效，其他情况为0。这说明R和θ独立，边缘概率密度分别为
f_R(r) = 2r，0 ≤ r ≤ 1，
f_θ(t) = 1/(2π)，0 ≤ t < 2π。

(c) 当X和Y独立同分布N(0,1)时，
联合概率密度函数为
f_{X,Y}(x, y) = (1 / 2π) e^{-(x² + y²)/2}，
因此g(r²) = (1 / 2π) e^{-r² / 2}。
联合概率密度函数为
f_{R,θ}(r, t) = r · (1 / 2π) e^{-r² / 2}。
这表示R和θ独立，边缘概率密度分别为
f_R(r) = r e^{-r² / 2}，r ≥ 0，
f_θ(t) = 1 / (2π)，0 ≤ t < 2π。
其中，R的分布称为韦布尔分布（Weibull）；注意它等价于随机变量W^{1/2}的分布，其中W ~ Expo(1/2)。

problems.titleZh: 作业 8 - 作业 8 第 7 题

problems.promptEn:
7. A network consists of n nodes, each pair of which may or may not have an edge
joining them. For example, a social network can be modeled as a group of n nodes
(representing people), where an edge between i and j means they know each other.
Assume the network is undirected and does not have edges from a node to itself (for
a social network, this says that if i knows j, then j knows i and that, contrary to
Socrates' advice, a person does not know himself or herself). A clique of size k is
a set of k nodes where every node has an edge to every other node (i.e., within the
clique, everyone knows everyone). An anticlique of size k is a set of k nodes where
there are no edges between them (i.e., within the anticlique, no one knows anyone
else). For example, the picture below shows a network with nodes labeled 1, 2, . . . , 7,
where {1, 2, 3, 4} is a clique of size 4, and {3, 5, 7} is an anticlique of size 3.
(a) Form a random network with n nodes by independently flipping fair coins to
decide for each pair {x, y} whether there is an edge joining them. Find the expected
number of cliques of size k (in terms of n and k).
(b) A triangle is a clique of size 3. For a random network as in (a), find the variance
of the number of triangles (in terms of n).
Hint: find the covariances of the indicator random variables for each possible clique.

problems.promptZh:
7. 一个网络由 n 个节点组成，每一对节点之间可能存在或不存在边连接它们。例如，一个社交网络可以建模为一组 n 个节点（代表人），其中 i 和 j 之间有边表示他们相互认识。假设网络是无向的，且不存在从节点自己到自己的边（对于社交网络，这意味着如果 i 认识 j，则 j 也认识 i；且与苏格拉底的建议相反，一个人不认识他自己）。大小为 k 的团（clique）是指一组 k 个节点，其中每个节点都与其他所有节点相连（即在该团内，人人相识）。大小为 k 的反团（anticlique）是一组 k 个节点，其中它们之间没有任何边（即在该反团内，没有人认识任何其他人）。例如，下图展示了一个网络，节点标记为 1, 2, ..., 7，其中 {1, 2, 3, 4} 是大小为 4 的团，而 {3, 5, 7} 是大小为 3 的反团。
(a) 通过对每一对 {x, y} 独立抛硬币决定它们之间是否存在边，构建一个随机网络。求大小为 k 的团的期望数量（用 n 和 k 表示）。
(b) 三角形是大小为 3 的团。对于如 (a) 中的随机网络，求三角形数量的方差（用 n 表示）。

problems.explanationZh:
7. 一个网络由 n 个节点组成，每一对节点之间可能存在或不存在边连接它们。例如，一个社交网络可以建模为一组 n 个节点（代表人），其中 i 和 j 之间有边表示他们相互认识。假设网络是无向的，且不存在从节点自己到自己的边（对于社交网络，这意味着如果 i 认识 j，则 j 也认识 i；且与苏格拉底的建议相反，一个人不认识他自己）。大小为 k 的团（clique）是指一组 k 个节点，其中每个节点都与其他所有节点相连（即在该团内，人人相识）。大小为 k 的反团（anticlique）是一组 k 个节点，其中它们之间没有任何边（即在该反团内，没有人认识任何其他人）。例如，下图展示了一个网络，节点标记为 1, 2, ..., 7，其中 {1, 2, 3, 4} 是大小为 4 的团，而 {3, 5, 7} 是大小为 3 的反团。

(a) 通过对每一对 {x, y} 独立抛公平硬币决定它们之间是否存在边，构建一个随机网络。求大小为 k 的团的期望数量（用 n 和 k 表示）。

将大小为 k 的子集（人群）以某种方式编号（即给每个大小为 k 的子集赋予一个编码），记 Xi 为相应的指示变量。因为 X1 + X2 + ··· + X_{ inom{n}{k}} 是大小为 k 的团的数量，所以期望为
E(X1 + X2 + ··· + X_{ inom{n}{k}}) =
 inom{n}{k} E(X1) =
 inom{n}{k} P(X1 = 1) =
 inom{n}{k} 2^{- inom{k}{2}}。

(b) 三角形是大小为 3 的团。对于如 (a) 中的随机网络，求三角形数量的方差（用 n 表示）。

提示：求各可能团的指示随机变量之间的协方差。共有
 inom{n}{3}
这样的指示随机变量，其中部分对相互独立，部分对相互依赖。

令 k=3，且 Xi 定义同 (a)。则

Var(X1 + ··· + X_{ inom{n}{3}}) = Var(X1) + ··· + Var(X_{ inom{n}{3}}) + 2\sum_{i<j} Cov(Xi, Xj)
=  inom{n}{3} Var(X1) + 2 \sum_{i<j} Cov(Xi, Xj)

我们有
Var(X1) = 2^{- inom{3}{2}} (1 - 2^{- inom{3}{2}}) = 7/64，因为伯努利(p) 随机变量的方差为 p(1 - p)。

计算 i<j 时的 Cov(Xi, Xj)，考虑组 i 和组 j 共有多少共同节点。如果共同人数为 0 或 1（如图 1 上方和左下方所示），则因为用于确定组 i 是否成团的硬币抛掷和组 j 独立，所以Cov(Xi, Xj) = 0；如果共同节点为 2（如图 1 右下方所示），则
Cov(Xi, Xj) = E(Xi Xj) - E(Xi)E(Xj) = 1/25 - (1/23)^2 = 1/64,

分别因为要使 Xi Xj=1，必须有5对不同节点间的边都存在。

一共有
 inom{n}{4} 	imes 6
组不相同的组对 {i,j} (i ≠ j) 有且仅有 1 对节点重叠（从 n 个节点选 4 个，再选其中两作为重叠）。其余组对的协方差为 0。因此，团数的方差为

 inom{n}{3} 	imes  rac{7}{64} + 2 	imes 6 	imes  inom{n}{4} 	imes  rac{1}{64} =  rac{7}{64} inom{n}{3} +  rac{3}{32}  inom{n}{4}。

(c) 假设
 inom{n}{k} < 2^{ inom{k}{2} - 1}。证明存在一个有 n 个节点的网络，不包含大小为 k 的团或大小为 k 的反团。

提示：说明为何只需证明随机网络满足该性质的概率大于零；然后考虑补集。

我们将通过证明随机网络满足该性质的概率为正来证明该网络的存在。令 A_i 表示第 i 个大小为 k 的节点组不是团且不是反团的事件。则有

P(\cup_{i=1}^{ inom{n}{k}} A_i^c) \leq \sum_{i=1}^{ inom{n}{k}} P(A_i^c) =  inom{n}{k} 2^{- inom{k}{2} + 1} < 1,

这说明

P(\cap_{i=1}^{ inom{n}{k}} A_i) = 1 - P(\cup_{i=1}^{ inom{n}{k}} A_i^c) > 0,

即存在一个网络满足题设条件。另一种方法是令 C 为大小为 k 的团数，A 为大小为 k 的反团数，T = C + A，则

E(T) = E(C) + E(A) =  inom{n}{k} 2^{- inom{k}{2} + 1} < 1

根据 (a) 部分的方法。若 P(T=0) = 0，则 P(T ≥ 1) = 1，进而 E(T) ≥ 1，矛盾。所以 P(T=0) > 0，说明存在无大小为 k 的团或反团的网络。

problems.bookName: Harvard Stat 110 概率论战略练习 Statistics 110 Strategic Practice
problems.promptEn:
1. Let B ⇠Beta(a, b). Find the distribution of 1 -B in two ways: (a) using a
change of variables and (b) using a story proof. Also explain why the result
makes sense in terms of Beta being the conjugate prior for the Binomial.
problems.promptZh: 1. 设 B ~ Beta(a, b)。请用两种方法找出 1 - B 的分布：（a）使用变量替换，（b）用故事证明。同时解释该结果为什么从Beta分布作为二项分布共轭先验的角度来看是合理的。
problems.explanation:
1. Let B ⇠Beta(a, b). Find the distribution of 1 -B in two ways: (a) using a
change of variables and (b) using a story proof. Also explain why the result
makes sense in terms of Beta being the conjugate prior for the Binomial.
(a) Let W = 1 -B. The function g(t) = 1 -t is strictly decreasing with
absolute derivative | -1| = 1, so the PDF of W is
fW(w) = Γ(a + b)
Γ(a)Γ(b)(1 -w)a-1wb-1,
for 0 < w < 1, which shows that W ⇠Beta(b, a).
(b) Using the bank-post oﬃce story, we can represent B =
X

Gamma(a, 1) and Y ⇠Gamma(b, 1) independent.
Then 1 -B =
Y
X+Y ⇠
Beta(b, a) by the same story.
This result makes sense intuitively since if we use Beta(a, b) as the prior dis-
tribution for the probability p of success in a Binomial problem, interpreting
a as the number of prior successes and b as the number of prior failures, then
1 -p is the probability of failure and, interchanging the roles of "success" and
"failure," it makes sense to have 1 -p ⇠Beta(b, a).

1. Let B ⇠Beta(a, b). Find the distribution of 1 -B in two ways: (a) using a
change of variables and (b) using a story proof. Also explain why the result
makes sense in terms of Beta being the conjugate prior for the Binomial.
(a) Let W = 1 -B. The function g(t) = 1 -t is strictly decreasing with
absolute derivative | -1| = 1, so the PDF of W is
fW(w) = Γ(a + b)
Γ(a)Γ(b)(1 -w)a-1wb-1,
for 0 < w < 1, which shows that W ⇠Beta(b, a).
(b) Using the bank-post oﬃce story, we can represent B =

Then 1 -B =
Y

This result makes sense intuitively since if we use Beta(a, b) as the prior dis-
tribution for the probability p of success in a Binomial problem, interpreting

1 -p is the probability of failure and, interchanging the roles of "success" and

problems.explanationZh:
1. 设 B ~ Beta(a, b)。请用两种方法找出 1 - B 的分布：（a）使用变量替换，（b）用故事证明。同时解释该结果为什么从Beta分布作为二项分布共轭先验的角度来看是合理的。

(a) 令 W = 1 - B。函数 g(t) = 1 - t 是严格递减的，导数的绝对值为 |-1| = 1，因此 W 的概率密度函数为

f_W(w) = \frac{\Gamma(a + b)}{\Gamma(a) \Gamma(b)} (1 - w)^{a - 1} w^{b - 1},

对 0 < w < 1 成立，由此可知 W ~ Beta(b, a)。

(b) 利用银行-邮局故事，我们可以将 B 表示为 \frac{X}{X + Y}，其中 X ~ Gamma(a, 1)，Y ~ Gamma(b, 1)，且两者独立。则

1 - B = \frac{Y}{X + Y} ~ Beta(b, a)，由同样的故事方法得出。

这个结果直观上是合理的，因为当我们用 Beta(a, b) 作为二项分布中成功概率 p 的先验分布时，a 表示先验成功次数，b 表示先验失败次数。令 1 - p 表示失败概率，交换“成功”和“失败”的角色，自然 1 - p ~ Beta(b, a)。

problems.explanationZh:
2. 设随机变量 X ~ Gamma(a, λ) 和 Y ~ Gamma(b, λ) 独立，且 a 和 b 是正整数。请通过三种方法证明 X + Y ~ Gamma(a + b, λ)：
(a) 使用卷积积分；
卷积积分为
f_{X+Y}(t) = ∫_0^t f_X(x) f_Y(t - x) dx = ∫_0^t \frac{λ^a}{Γ(a)} x^{a-1} e^{-λx} \frac{λ^b}{Γ(b)} (t - x)^{b-1} e^{-λ(t - x)} dx，
由于积分区间需满足 x > 0 且 t - x > 0，因此积分上下限为0到 t。
整理得
f_{X+Y}(t) = \frac{λ^{a+b} e^{-λ t}}{Γ(a) Γ(b)} ∫_0^t x^{a-1} (t - x)^{b-1} dx。
令 u = x / t，则积分区间可变为 0 到 1，且
∫_0^1 u^{a-1} (1 - u)^{b-1} du = B(a, b) = \frac{Γ(a) Γ(b)}{Γ(a+b)}（贝塔积分），
从而得到
f_{X+Y}(t) = \frac{λ^{a+b} e^{-λ t} t^{a + b - 1}}{Γ(a + b)},
即 X + Y ~ Gamma(a + b, λ)。
(b) 使用矩母函数（MGF）；
由于 X 和 Y 独立，有
M_{X+Y}(t) = M_X(t) M_Y(t) = \left( \frac{λ}{λ - t} \right)^a \left( \frac{λ}{λ - t} \right)^b = \left( \frac{λ}{λ - t} \right)^{a + b},
而这正是 Gamma(a + b, λ) 的矩母函数，故 X + Y ~ Gamma(a + b, λ)。
(c) 使用故事证明；
将 X 解释为速率为 λ 的泊松过程中的第 a 次到达时间，将 Y 解释为再等待 b 次到达的时间（独立于 X，因为连续间隔为参数为 λ 的指数分布独立随机变量）。则 X + Y 是第 a + b 次到达时间，故 X + Y ~ Gamma(a + b, λ)。

problems.explanationZh:
3. Fred 等待上班的公交时间为 X ~ Gamma(a, λ) 分钟，下班回家时等待公交时间为 Y ~ Gamma(b, λ) 分钟，且 X 和 Y 独立。问：比例 X/Y 是否与总等待时间 X + Y 独立？
如银行与邮局故事中所示，定义 W = X / (X + Y) 与总等待时间 X + Y 独立，
因此 W 的任意函数均与 X + Y 独立。而 X/Y 可表示为
X / Y = \frac{X / (X + Y)}{Y / (X + Y)} = \frac{W}{1 - W},
是 W 的函数。因此，X/Y 与 X + Y 独立。

4. F检验是一个非常广泛使用的统计检验，基于 F(m, n) 分布，该分布是
X/m
Y/n 的分布，其中 X ⇠ Gamma(m/2, 1/2)，Y ⇠ Gamma(n/2, 1/2)。
求当 V ⇠ F(m, n) 时，随机变量 mV/(n + mV) 的分布。
设 X ⇠ Gamma(m/2, 1/2)，Y ⇠ Gamma(n/2, 1/2)，且 V = (n/m)(X/Y)。则
mV/(n + mV) = (nX/Y) / (n + nX/Y) = X / (X + Y) ⇠ Beta(m/2, n/2)。

problems.explanationZh:
1. 设 U1, ..., Un 是独立同分布的 Unif(0, 1) 随机变量。求第 j 个次序统计量 U(j) 的概率密度函数（包括归一化常数），以及它的期望和方差。
课堂上已证明 U(j) ⇠ Beta(j, n - j + 1)，因此 U(j) 的概率密度函数为
f(t) =
Γ(n + 1) / [Γ(j)Γ(n - j + 1)] * t^{j-1} * (1 - t)^{n - j},
其中 0 < t < 1，其他情况下为 0。利用阶乘函数表示即为
f(t) = n! / [(j-1)! (n-j)!] * t^{j-1} (1 - t)^{n-j}。

Beta 分布 Beta(a, b) 的期望为 a/(a + b)。
计算方差时，先求二阶矩：
E(T^2) = (a + 1)a / [(a + b + 1)(a + b)]，利用 Γ 函数性质 Γ(x+1) = xΓ(x) 和积分 Beta 分布密度得出。

因此方差为
Var(T) = E(T^2) - [E(T)]^2 = ab / [(a + b)^2 (a + b + 1)] = μ(1 - μ) / (a + b + 1)，其中 μ = a/(a + b)。

所以，U(j) 的期望为 μ = j / (n + 1)，方差为
j (n - j + 1) / [(n + 1)^2 (n + 2)]。

problems.explanationZh:
2. 设 X 和 Y 是相互独立的 Expo(λ) 随机变量，且 M = max(X, Y)。用两种方法证明 M 和 X + 1/2 Y 具有相同的分布：

(a) M 的分布函数是
FM(x) = P(M ≤ x) = P(X ≤ x, Y ≤ x) = (1 - e^{-λx})^2，
而 X + 1/2 Y 的分布函数是
FX+1/2Y(x) = P(X + 1/2 Y ≤ x) = ∬_{s + 1/2 t ≤ x} λ^2 e^{-λs} e^{-λt} ds dt

= ∫_0^{2x} λ e^{-λ t} dt ∫_0^{x - 1/2 t} λ e^{-λ s} ds
= ∫_0^{2x} (1 - e^{-λ(x - 1/2 t)}) λ e^{-λ t} dt = (1 - e^{-λ x})^2。

因此，M 和 X + 1/2 Y 的分布函数相同。

(b) 这类似于“3个学生解决作业”的问题，不过这里是2个学生。令 L = min(X, Y)，则 M = L + (M - L)。在该问题中（2个学生版），L ~ Expo(2λ)，表示第一个学生完成作业所用时间；根据无记忆性质，第二个学生完成作业的额外时间 M - L  ~ Expo(λ)，且与 L 独立。由于 2Y ~ Expo(2λ) 并且独立于 X ~ Expo(λ)，故 M = L + (M - L) 与 1/2 Y + X 有相同分布。

problems.explanationZh:
3. 设 X1, X2, ..., Xn 是具有分布函数 F 和概率密度函数 f 的独立同分布随机变量。求顺序统计量 X(i) 和 X(j)（1 ≤ i < j ≤ n）的联合概率密度函数。

设在实轴上取两个点 a < b。为了让 X(i) 落在 a 附近的一个微小区间，而 X(j) 落在 b 附近的一个微小区间，需要满足：

- 有 1 个 X_k ≈ a，
- 有 1 个 X_k ≈ b，
- 有 i - 1 个 X_k ≤ a，
- 有 n - j 个 X_k ≥ b，
- 剩余的 j - i - 1 个 X_k 在 (a, b) 之间。

根据上述分类，联合概率密度函数为

f_{(i),(j)}(a,b) =
\frac{n!}{(i - 1)! (j - i - 1)! (n - j)!}
\times F(a)^{i - 1} f(a) [F(b) - F(a)]^{j - i - 1} f(b) [1 - F(b)]^{n - j},

其中系数是将 n 个 X_k 分配到“a 左侧”、“a 附近”、“a 与 b 之间”、“b 附近”、“b 右侧”五个类别的组合数，等同于多项式概率的系数。也可以写成组合形式

n (n-1) \binom{n-2}{i-1} \binom{n-i-1}{j-i-1}

因为首先选择哪个 X_k 附近是 a，再选择哪一个附近是 b，依此类推。

problems.titleZh: 专项练习 9 - 条件期望 #1

classificationReview.logic: Harvard Stat 110 is a probability course. Difficulty is inferred from set number, homework/strategic role, proof wording, and extracted prompt/solution length.

problems.bookName: Harvard Stat 110 概率论战略练习 Statistics 110 Strategic Practice
problems.promptEn:
1. You get to choose between two envelopes, each of which contains a check for
some positive amount of money. Unlike in the two envelope paradox from class,

it is not given that one envelope contains twice as much money as the other
envelope. Instead, assume that the two values were generated independently
from some distribution on positive real numbers, with no information given
about what that distribution is.
After picking an envelope, you can open it and see how much money is inside
(call this value x), and then you have the option of switching. As no information
has been given about the distribution, it may seem impossible to have better
than a 50% chance of picking the better envelope. Intuitively, we may want
to switch if x is "small" and not switch if x is "large," but how do we define
"small" and "large" in the grand scheme of all possible distributions? [The last
sentence was a rhetorical question.]
Consider the following strategy for deciding whether to switch. Generate a
"threshold" T ⇠Expo(1), and switch envelopes if and only if the observed
value x is less than the value of T. Show that this strategy succeeds in picking
the envelope with more money with probability strictly greater than 1/2.
Hint: Let t be the value of T (generated by a random draw from the Expo(1)
distribution). First explain why the strategy works very well if t happens to be
in between the two envelope values, and does no harm in any case (i.e., there is
no case in which the strategy succeeds with probability strictly less than 1/2).
problems.promptZh:
1. 你可以在两个信封中选择一个，每个信封里都有一张支票，金额均为正数。与课堂上讲的两个信封悖论不同，这里没有给定其中一个信封的金额是另一个的两倍。相反，假设两个金额是独立从某个关于正实数的分布中生成的，且对该分布没有任何信息。

选中一个信封后，你可以打开看里面有多少钱（记作 x），然后你可以选择是否换另一个信封。由于对分布一无所知，似乎不可能有超过 50% 以上的概率选到金额更大的信封。直觉上，我们可能想当 x “较小”时选择换信封，而 x “较大”时不换，但如何在所有可能的分布中定义“较小”和“较大”呢？【最后一句是设问句。】

考虑以下决定是否换信封的策略：生成一个阈值 T ~ Expo(1)（指数分布参数为1），当且仅当观察到的 x 小于 T 时换信封。证明该策略成功选到金额更大的信封的概率严格大于 1/2。

提示：设 t 为指数分布生成的阈值 T。首先说明当 t 恰好位于两个信封金额之间时，该策略表现非常好；且该策略不会带来任何损害（即不存在策略成功概率严格小于 1/2 的情况）。
problems.explanation:
1. You get to choose between two envelopes, each of which contains a check for
some positive amount of money. Unlike in the two envelope paradox from class,
it is not given that one envelope contains twice as much money as the other
envelope. Instead, assume that the two values were generated independently
from some distribution on positive real numbers, with no information given
about what that distribution is.
After picking an envelope, you can open it and see how much money is inside
(call this value x), and then you have the option of switching. As no information
has been given about the distribution, it may seem impossible to have better
than a 50% chance of picking the better envelope. Intuitively, we may want
to switch if x is "small" and not switch if x is "large," but how do we define
"small" and "large" in the grand scheme of all possible distributions? [The last
sentence was a rhetorical question.]
Consider the following strategy for deciding whether to switch. Generate a
"threshold" T ⇠Expo(1), and switch envelopes if and only if the observed
value x is less than the value of T. Show that this strategy succeeds in picking
the envelope with more money with probability strictly greater than 1/2.
Hint: Let t be the value of T (generated by a random draw from the Expo(1)
distribution). First explain why the strategy works very well if t happens to be
in between the two envelope values, and does no harm in any case (i.e., there is
no case in which the strategy succeeds with probability strictly less than 1/2).
Let a be the smaller value of the two envelopes and b be the larger value (assume
a < b since in the case a = b it makes no dierence which envelope is chosen!).
Let G be the event that the strategy succeeds and A be the event that we pick
the envelope with a initially. Then P(G|A) = P(T > a) = 1 -(1 -e-a) = e-a,
and P(G|Ac) = P(T b) = 1 -e-b. Thus, the probability that the strategy
succeeds is
2e-a + 1
2(1 -e-b) = 1
2 + 1
2(e-a -e-b) > 1
2,
because e-a -e-b > 0.

2e-a + 1
2(1 -e-b) = 1

2(e-a -e-b) > 1

problems.explanationZh:
1. 你可以在两个信封中选择一个，每个信封里都有一张支票，金额均为正数。与课堂上讲的两个信封悖论不同，这里没有给定其中一个信封的金额是另一个的两倍。相反，假设两个金额是独立从某个关于正实数的分布中生成的，且对该分布没有任何信息。

选中一个信封后，你可以打开看里面有多少钱（记作 x），然后你可以选择是否换另一个信封。由于对分布一无所知，似乎不可能有超过 50% 以上的概率选到金额更大的信封。直觉上，我们可能想当 x “较小”时选择换信封，而 x “较大”时不换，但如何在所有可能的分布中定义“较小”和“较大”呢？【最后一句是设问句。】

考虑以下决定是否换信封的策略：生成一个阈值 T ~ Expo(1)（指数分布参数为1），当且仅当观察到的 x 小于 T 时换信封。证明该策略成功选到金额更大的信封的概率严格大于 1/2。

提示：设 t 为指数分布生成的阈值 T。首先说明当 t 恰好位于两个信封金额之间时，该策略表现非常好；且该策略不会带来任何损害（即不存在策略成功概率严格小于 1/2 的情况）。

设 a 为两个信封中较小的金额，b 为较大的金额（假设 a < b，因为若 a = b，选哪个信封都无差别）。令 G 表示策略成功的事件，A 表示我们最初选到了金额为 a 的信封。则：

P(G|A) = P(T > a) = 1 - (1 - e^{-a}) = e^{-a},

且

P(G|A^c) = P(T ≤ b) = 1 - e^{-b}.

因此策略成功的总概率为

(1/2) * e^{-a} + (1/2) * (1 - e^{-b}) = 1/2 + (1/2)(e^{-a} - e^{-b}) > 1/2,

因为 e^{-a} - e^{-b} > 0（因为 a < b，指数衰减函数单调递减）。

problems.titleZh: 专项练习 9 - 条件期望 #2

classificationReview.logic: Harvard Stat 110 is a probability course. Difficulty is inferred from set number, homework/strategic role, proof wording, and extracted prompt/solution length.

problems.bookName: Harvard Stat 110 概率论战略练习 Statistics 110 Strategic Practice
problems.promptEn: 2. Give a short, direct proof that a Geom(p) r.v. has mean q/p, for q = 1 -p.
problems.promptZh: 2. 给出关于几何分布 Geom(p) 的随机变量均值为 q/p 的简短直接证明，其中 q = 1 - p。
problems.explanation:
2. Give a short, direct proof that a Geom(p) r.v. has mean q/p, for q = 1 -p.
Let X ⇠Geom(p), interpreted as the number of failures before the first success
in independent Bern(p) trials. Let A be the event that the first trial is a success.

Then
E(X) = E(X|A)P(A) + E(X|Ac)P(Ac) = 0p + (1 + E(X))q
yields E(X) = q/p.
problems.explanationEn:
2. Give a short, direct proof that a Geom(p) r.v. has mean q/p, for q = 1 -p.
Let X ⇠Geom(p), interpreted as the number of failures before the first success
in independent Bern(p) trials. Let A be the event that the first trial is a success.

Then
E(X) = E(X|A)P(A) + E(X|Ac)P(Ac) = 0p + (1 + E(X))q
yields E(X) = q/p.
problems.explanationZh:
2. 给出关于几何分布 Geom(p) 的随机变量均值为 q/p 的简短直接证明，其中 q = 1 - p。

设 X ~ Geom(p)，其中 X 表示独立 Bern(p) 试验中首次成功之前的失败次数。令事件 A 表示第一次试验成功。

则

E(X) = E(X | A)P(A) + E(X | A^c)P(A^c) = 0 * p + (1 + E(X)) * q,

其中 E(X | A) = 0（第一次试验即成功，因此失败次数为0），事件 A 发生概率为 p；

事件 A^c（首次试验失败）发生时，失败次数为 1（本次失败）加上之后失败次数的期望 E(X)。

解此方程得

E(X) = q / p.

each day (without repetitions within the choice of 5 numbers). Suppose that

problems.explanationZh:
3. “Mass Cash” 彩票每天会随机选择1, 2, ..., 35中的5个号码（选择的5个号码之间无重复）。假设我们想知道直到所有号码都被选中需要多长时间。令 aj 为当我们还缺少 j 个号码时，所需的平均额外天数（所以 a0 = 0，a35 是收集完全部35个号码的平均所需天数）。求 aj 的递推公式。
假设我们还缺 j 个号码（0 ≤ j ≤ 35），令 Tj 为完成收集还需的额外天数。条件于第二天出现多少个“新”号码，记为 N。由此得到
E(Tj) = ∑_{n=0} E(Tj | N = n) P(N = n)。
注意 N 服从超几何分布（可想象为在数字中标记那些我们还未收集的号码）！令 ak = 0 对所有 k < 0， 则有
aj = 1 + ∑_{n=0} aj-n · (C(j, n) · C(35-j, 5-n)) / C(35, 5)。

problems.explanationZh:
4. 你获得了一个绝佳机会去竞标一个内含神秘奖品的神秘箱子！奖品的价值完全未知，但至少为零，最多为一百万美元。所以奖品的真正价值 V 被视为均匀分布在 [0,1] 上（单位为百万美元）。你可以选择投标任意金额 b（单位为百万美元）。你有机会以远低于奖品价值的价格获得奖品，但若出价过高也可能亏钱。具体而言，如果 b < 2/3 V，则投标被拒绝，既无收益也无损失。如果 b ≥ 2/3 V，则投标成功，你的净收益为 V - b（因为你支付了 b 得到价值为 V 的奖品）。问：你的最优投标 b 是多少（以最大化期望收益）？
我们选择一个 b ≥ 0 的投标额，这个值不能根据未知的 V 来定义。期望收益可以通过条件概率求得，即条件于投标是否被接受。投标被拒绝的期望收益是 0，故期望收益为
E(V - b | b ≥ 2/3 V) · P(b ≥ 2/3 V) = (E(V | V ≤ 3/2 b) - b) · P(V ≤ 3/2 b)。
当 b ≥ 2/3 时，投标必然接受，但平均会亏钱，因此假设 b < 2/3。则
(E(V | V ≤ 3/2 b) - b) · P(V ≤ 3/2 b) = (3/4 b - b) · 3/2 b = -3/8 b²，
这里条件分布 V | V ≤ 3/2 b 是在区间 [0, 3/2 b] 上的均匀分布。
上式在 b=0 时最大且非负，其他 b 都为负，因此最优投标是 0：不应参与此游戏！这个故事的启示是什么？第一，在完全无价值信息的前提下投资某资产是一种糟糕的决定。第二，要基于所有可用信息进行条件统计。在上述计算中，使用条件期望 E(V | V ≤ 3/2 b) 而非无条件期望 E(V) = 1/2 是关键；投标被接受本身就提供了奖品可能价值的额外信息！

problems.promptZh:
1. 设 X 和 Y 是独立的正的随机变量，且期望值有限。
(a) 给出一个例子，使得 E( X / (X + Y) ) ≠ E(X) / E(X + Y)，并且精确计算两边的值。
提示：从你能想到的最简单的例子开始！

Hint: start by thinking about the simplest examples you can think of!
As a simple example, let X take on the values 1 and 3 with probability 1/2 each, and
let Y take on the values 3 and 5 with probability 1/2 each. Then E(X)/E(X +Y ) =
2/(2 + 4) = 1/3, but E(X/(X + Y )) = 31/96 (the average of the 4 possible values of
X/(X + Y ), which are equally likely). An even simpler example is to let X be the
constant 1 (a degenerate r.v.), and let Y be 1 or 3 with probability 1/2 each. Then
E(X)/E(X + Y ) = 1/(1 + 2) = 1/3, but E(X/(X + Y )) = 3/8.

problems.explanationZh:
1. 设 X 和 Y 是独立的正的随机变量，且期望值有限。
(a) 给出一个例子，使得 E( X / (X + Y) ) ≠ E(X) / E(X + Y)，并且精确计算两边的值。
提示：从你能想到的最简单的例子开始！
一个简单的例子是令 X 以概率各 1/2 取值 1 和 3，令 Y 以概率各 1/2 取值 3 和 5。则 E(X)/E(X+Y) = 2 / (2 + 4) = 1/3，但 E(X/(X+Y)) = 31/96（这是 X/(X+Y) 四个可能值的均值，等概率）。一个更简单的例子是令 X 为常数 1（退化随机变量），Y 以概率各 1/2 取值 1 和 3，则 E(X)/E(X+Y) = 1/(1+2) = 1/3，但 E(X/(X+Y)) = 3/8。

(b) 如果 X 和 Y 是独立同分布（i.i.d.），那么是否一定有 E( X / (X + Y) ) = E(X) / E(X + Y)？
是的。由于对称性，E(X/(X+Y)) = E(Y/(X+Y))，因此
E(X/(X+Y)) + E(Y/(X+Y)) = E((X+Y)/(X+Y)) = 1,
所以 E(X/(X+Y)) = 1/2。另一方面
E(X)/E(X+Y) = E(X) / (E(X) + E(Y)) = E(X) / (E(X) + E(X)) = 1/2。

(c) 现在设 X 服从 Gamma(a, λ) 分布，Y 服从 Gamma(b, λ) 分布。在不使用微积分的情况下证明，对任意实数 c > 0，有 E( X^c / (X + Y)^c ) = E(X^c) / E((X + Y)^c)。
此等式等价于随机变量 X^c / (X + Y)^c 和 (X + Y)^c 不相关。课堂上通过银行窗口的故事证明了 X / (X + Y) 与 X + Y 独立，因此 X^c / (X + Y)^c 与 (X + Y)^c 独立，从而它们不相关。

problems.bookName: Harvard Stat 110 概率论战略练习 Statistics 110 Strategic Practice
problems.promptEn:
2. The Gumbel distribution is the distribution of -log X with X ⇠Expo(1).
(a) Find the CDF of the Gumbel distribution.
(b) Let X1, X2, . . . be i.i.d. Expo(1) and let Mn = max(X1, . . . , Xn). Show that
Mn -log n converges in distribution to the Gumbel distribution, i.e., as n ! 1 the
CDF of Mn -log n converges to the Gumbel CDF.
problems.promptZh:
2. Gumbel 分布是随机变量 -log X，其中 X 服从 Expo(1) 分布。
(a) 求 Gumbel 分布的分布函数（CDF）。
(b) 令 X1, X2, ... 是 i.i.d. 的 Expo(1) 分布随机变量，令 Mn = max(X1, ..., Xn)。证明 Mn - log n 在分布上收敛于 Gumbel 分布，即当 n → ∞ 时，Mn - log n 的分布函数收敛至 Gumbel 分布的分布函数。
problems.explanation:
2. The Gumbel distribution is the distribution of -log X with X ⇠Expo(1).
(a) Find the CDF of the Gumbel distribution.
Let G be Gumbel and X ⇠Expo(1). The CDF is
P(G t) = P(-log X t) = P(X ≥e-t) = e-e-t
for all real t.

(b) Let X1, X2, . . . be i.i.d. Expo(1) and let Mn = max(X1, . . . , Xn). Show that
Mn -log n converges in distribution to the Gumbel distribution, i.e., as n ! 1 the
CDF of Mn -log n converges to the Gumbel CDF.
The CDF of Mn -log n is
P(Mn -log n t) = P(X1 t + log n, . . . , Xn t + log n) = P(X1 t + log n)n.
Using the Expo CDF and the fact that (1 + x
n)n ! ex as n ! 1, this becomes
(1 -e-(t+log n))n = (1 -e-t
n )n ! e-e-t.
problems.explanationEn:
2. The Gumbel distribution is the distribution of -log X with X ⇠Expo(1).
(a) Find the CDF of the Gumbel distribution.
Let G be Gumbel and X ⇠Expo(1). The CDF is
P(G t) = P(-log X t) = P(X ≥e-t) = e-e-t
for all real t.

(b) Let X1, X2, . . . be i.i.d. Expo(1) and let Mn = max(X1, . . . , Xn). Show that
Mn -log n converges in distribution to the Gumbel distribution, i.e., as n ! 1 the
CDF of Mn -log n converges to the Gumbel CDF.
The CDF of Mn -log n is
P(Mn -log n t) = P(X1 t + log n, . . . , Xn t + log n) = P(X1 t + log n)n.
Using the Expo CDF and the fact that (1 + x
n)n ! ex as n ! 1, this becomes
(1 -e-(t+log n))n = (1 -e-t
n )n ! e-e-t.
problems.explanationZh:
2. Gumbel 分布是随机变量 -log X，其中 X 服从 Expo(1) 分布。
(a) 求 Gumbel 分布的分布函数（CDF）。
设 G 为 Gumbel 随机变量，X 服从 Expo(1) 分布。则其分布函数为
P(G ≤ t) = P(-log X ≤ t) = P(X ≥ e^{-t}) = e^{-e^{-t}}
对所有实数 t 均成立。

(b) 令 X1, X2, ... 是 i.i.d. 的 Expo(1) 分布随机变量，令 Mn = max(X1, ..., Xn)。证明 Mn - log n 在分布上收敛于 Gumbel 分布，即当 n → ∞ 时，Mn - log n 的分布函数收敛至 Gumbel 分布的分布函数。
Mn - log n 的分布函数为
P(Mn - log n ≤ t) = P(X1 ≤ t + log n, ..., Xn ≤ t + log n) = [P(X1 ≤ t + log n)]^n。
利用 Expo(1) 的分布函数及极限 (1 + x/n)^n → e^x，当 n → ∞，得到
(1 - e^{-(t + log n)})^n = (1 - e^{-t}/n)^n → e^{-e^{-t}}。
